findings.title: EmailChangeRevertForm submit button has no Loader2 spinner during submission
findings.evidence: src/components/auth/email-change-revert-form.tsx:103-111 — the destructive Button renders t('submitting') text only when state.kind === 'submitting', with no Loader2Icon + motion-safe:animate-spin. All peer auth forms (sign-in-form, forgot-password-form, reset-password-form, change-password-form, invite-redeem-form) include the spinner icon.
findings.recommendation: Add import Loader2Icon from lucide-react and add the same Loader2Icon + motion-safe:animate-spin pattern used in the other auth forms inside the submitting state branch of the revert button.

findings.title: lockout_cleared cron not registered in vercel.json — SC-004 broken in production
findings.evidence: vercel.json:1-17 — crons array contains only 3 entries (outbox-purge, sweep-stale-pending-refunds, receipt-pdf-reconcile). The lockout-cleanup route at src/app/api/cron/lockout-cleanup/route.ts:10 is fully implemented and emits `lockout_cleared` audit events, but is not scheduled. The domain audit-event.ts:49 and schema.ts:59 both declare `lockout_cleared`. Grep confirms the ONLY callsite that emits `lockout_cleared` is the cron route (src/app/api/cron/lockout-cleanup/route.ts:100) — the sign-in path (sign-in.ts:350) emits `sign_in_success` + clears the count but never emits `lockout_cleared`. SC-004 requires 100% of 16 audit event types to be captured; without the cron running this type is permanently silent in production.
findings.recommendation: Add the lockout-cleanup cron to vercel.json: { "path": "/api/cron/lockout-cleanup", "schedule": "*/15 * * * *" }. The route, auth guard, and audit logic are already correct. This is a one-line vercel.json fix. Note: functional lockout enforcement (FR-013) is NOT broken — sign-in.ts checks `isLocked(user, now)` inline at DB read time, so users are blocked then unblocked correctly without the cron. Only the audit trail and DB cleanup are impacted.

findings.title: forgot-password: token invalidation not atomic with token creation — user can lose reset link on crash
findings.evidence: src/modules/auth/application/forgot-password.ts:161-165 — `invalidateAllUnconsumedForUser` (line 161) and `createReset` (line 162) are two sequential top-level awaits with no transaction. A crash between them leaves all prior tokens invalidated and no new token created. The user never receives an email, has no live reset link, and must submit the form again.

findings.title: upstash-rate-limiter peek(): Upstash getRemaining() return-value cast is speculative and error-prone
findings.evidence: src/modules/auth/infrastructure/rate-limit/upstash-rate-limiter.ts:196-205 — `getRateLimit(max, windowSeconds).getRemaining(key)` is assigned to `remaining`. The subsequent type narrowing uses two nested `typeof remaining === 'object' && 'reset' in remaining` / `'remaining' in remaining` guards with `as { reset: number }` / `as { remaining: number }` casts, falling back to `remaining as unknown as number`. This is speculative — the Upstash SDK's `getRemaining` return type is not `{ reset, remaining }` in the documented API surface (it returns a `RatelimitResponse`-shaped object). If the SDK changes its return shape, the fallback `remaining as unknown as number` silently makes `remainingCount` whatever the object is, causing `success: remainingCount > 0` to evaluate to `success: true` unconditionally (truthy object). The in-memory fallback path (line 178) is correct.

findings.title: enable-user: users.enable() and audit.append() are not atomic — brief inconsistent-state window
findings.evidence: src/modules/auth/application/enable-user.ts:60-68 — `deps.users.enable(target.id)` (line 60) commits the status='active' change, then `deps.audit.append(...)` (line 62) fires. The audit repo's never-throws contract (A1) means if the DB insert for the audit row fails, it is silently swallowed. For re-enable this is acceptable (enable is idempotent, audit repo logs the miss to pino + metric). However, if a crash occurs AFTER `enable` commits but before `deps.users.findById(target.id)` returns (line 71), the caller gets `Err(not-found)` (line 72 expression `updated ?? target` is actually `ok({ user: target })` — let me note this returns `target` on null, which is the pre-update row). This means the route handler sees `user.status === 'disabled'` in the returned domain object, but the DB has `status = 'active'`. Minor inconsistency in the response, not the DB.
findings.recommendation: Replace the fallback `updated ?? target` with a throw if `updated` is null (should never happen on a just-updated row). For full atomicity wrap in db.transaction the same way disable-user should be fixed. Minimal fix: `if (!updated) throw new Error('enable-user: read-after-write returned null'); return ok({ user: updated });`

findings.title: disableUser / changeRole accept any cross-tenant userId with no tenant-ownership verification
findings.evidence: src/modules/auth/application/disable-user.ts:74 — `deps.users.findById(input.targetUserId)` queries the global cross-tenant `users` table with no tenant predicate. src/modules/auth/application/change-role.ts:63 — same. Route handlers at src/app/api/auth/users/[id]/disable/route.ts:19 and src/app/api/auth/users/[id]/role/route.ts:39 pass the raw URL `[id]` param directly as `asUserId(id)` with no membership/contact check. Under the current MTA+STD (single-tenant) deployment the `users` table holds only one tenant's accounts, so no cross-tenant actor exists — not exploitable today.
findings.recommendation: Before calling `disableUser` / `changeRole` in the route handler, verify the target user belongs to the actor's tenant. The simplest approach: add a `UserRepo.findByIdInTenant(id, tenantSlug)` helper that JOINs `users` with `members` (via `contacts`) for the tenant, returning null if not found. Return 404 on null to avoid leaking cross-tenant existence. Tag this as a mandatory F10 pre-condition in `docs/phases-plan.md`. Test: integration test asserting POST `/api/auth/users/<tenant-b-user>/disable` from a tenant-A admin session returns 404.

findings.title: reissueInvitation emits no F1 audit event — silent path if called outside F3
findings.evidence: src/modules/auth/application/reissue-invitation.ts:46-53 — JSDoc explicitly states 'Audit: this F1 primitive emits NO audit event of its own. The chamber-facing trail is owned by the F3 caller, which emits `member_portal_invite_queued`.' The tx block at lines 123-175 contains no `deps.audit.appendInTx` call. Only a `logger.info` breadcrumb at line 179.
findings.recommendation: Add an `account_invitation_resent` audit event type (new migration) and emit it inside the `reissueInvitation` tx after the outbox enqueue. Alternatively, promote the F3 `member_portal_invite_queued` emission into the F1 primitive's tx and remove the coupling. Test: unit test that asserts `audit.appendInTx` is called once when `reissueInvitation` succeeds.

findings.title: change-role / disable-user countActiveAdmins uses pool-global db outside any transaction
findings.evidence: src/modules/auth/application/change-role.ts:80-84 — calls `deps.users.countActiveAdmins()` then `deps.users.setRole()` as two separate operations with no enclosing transaction. src/modules/auth/application/disable-user.ts:82-86 — same pattern. src/modules/auth/infrastructure/db/user-repo.ts:263-269 — `countActiveAdmins` uses `db` (pool-global), not a tx handle.
findings.recommendation: No action required for go-live. For future hardening: move the `countActiveAdmins` check inside a `db.transaction` together with `setRole` / `disable` and acquire a `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` advisory lock to remove the explicit dependency on the DB trigger as a safety net. Document this in a follow-up tech-debt ticket.

findings.title: Password-strength bar uses bg-emerald-500 / bg-amber-500 instead of --success / --warning tokens
findings.evidence: src/components/auth/password-strength.tsx:90 (`return 'bg-emerald-500'`) and :92 (`return 'bg-amber-500'`). The 'weak' and 'empty' segments correctly use the semantic `bg-destructive` (:94) and `bg-muted` (:97), so the component is half-tokenized — only the strong/acceptable colors bypass the --success (globals.css:165) and --warning (:173) tokens that exist precisely for this.
findings.recommendation: Change barColour() to `case 'strong': return 'bg-success'` and `case 'acceptable': return 'bg-warning'` to align with the destructive/muted cases already in the same switch. One-line-each change; the warning token is L-tuned (globals.css:173) so the amber stays visually close.

findings.title: InviteUserDialog locked-member display uses role=textbox on a non-focusable div
findings.evidence: src/components/auth/invite-user-dialog.tsx:277-284 — <div role="textbox" aria-readonly="true" aria-labelledby="invite-link-member-label"> has no tabIndex, making it unreachable by keyboard. ARIA 1.2 spec requires that an element with role=textbox be focusable (it inherits the interactive widget contract). The element participates in the form's visual layout but is keyboard-invisible.
findings.recommendation: Replace role="textbox" with role="status" (or remove role entirely and leave as a plain descriptive <div>) since this element is read-only display, not an editable widget. A label + aria-labelledby pairing still works without the widget role. If interactive-role semantics are desired, add tabIndex={0}.

findings.title: PasswordStrength bar text is text-muted-foreground at 12px — marginal contrast in light mode
findings.evidence:
src/components/auth/password-strength.tsx:121 —

renders strength label text at 12px (var(--font-size-caption) = 0.75rem). In light mode --muted-foreground is oklch(0.515 0 0) ≈ #767676 which gives approximately 4.54:1 against white #ffffff background — barely passing WCAG AA for normal text at ≥14px or bold ≥18px, but 12px falls below the 14px/bold threshold. The contrast is right at the edge and may fail under AA audit tools that classify 12px regular as small text requiring 4.5:1.
findings.recommendation: Increase the strength label to text-sm (14px) by changing the class from text-xs to text-sm on the label paragraph, which moves it into the 'normal text ≥14px' category where --muted-foreground passes cleanly.

findings.title: UsersFilters 'Clear filters' button: XIcon not aria-hidden — redundant SR announcement
findings.evidence: src/components/auth/users-filters.tsx:153 — <XIcon className="size-4" /> has no aria-hidden attribute. The button has visible text 'Clear filters' (from t('clear')). Without aria-hidden on the icon, SR announces 'X Clear filters button' or 'X image Clear filters button' depending on the browser+SR pair. Every other icon in the auth module and adjacent filter components uses aria-hidden on decorative icons.
findings.recommendation: Add aria-hidden to the XIcon: <XIcon className="size-4" aria-hidden />. Consistent with all other lucide icons in the auth UI.

findings.title: invite-redeem-form and reset-password-form linkInvalid error cards have no managed focus on mount
findings.evidence: src/components/auth/invite-redeem-form.tsx:149-165 and src/components/auth/reset-password-form.tsx:142-157 — when linkInvalid becomes true and the form swaps to the error card via conditional render, focus remains wherever it was (typically the submit button that just disappeared from the DOM). The new error card with role="alert" will be announced by live region, but keyboard focus is now on an unmounted element, which browsers typically drop to the document body.
findings.recommendation: Add a useRef on the error card container and a useEffect that calls ref.current.focus() when linkInvalid transitions to true. Add tabIndex={-1} on the error container div so it is programmatically focusable without entering the tab sequence.

findings.title: disableUser and changeRole each issue a trailing read-after-write findById adding 1 extra DB RTT per operation
findings.evidence: src/modules/auth/application/disable-user.ts:123 — `const updated = await deps.users.findById(target.id)` called after `deps.users.disable()` succeeds. src/modules/auth/application/change-role.ts:124 — `const updated = await deps.users.findById(target.id)` called after `deps.users.setRole()` succeeds. Both operations already hold the pre-mutation `target: UserAccount` object; the `disable()` and `setRole()` calls perform a single-column UPDATE that changes exactly one known field (`status` → `'disabled'`, or `role` → `input.newRole`). The returned `updated` could be constructed as `{ ...target, status: 'disabled' }` / `{ ...target, role: input.newRole }` without a round-trip.
findings.recommendation: In Application layer (`disable-user.ts`, `change-role.ts`): replace the post-mutation `findById` with a local domain object construction. For `disableUser`: `return ok({ user: { ...target, status: 'disabled' }, sessionsRevoked })`. For `changeRole`: `return ok({ user: { ...target, role: input.newRole }, sessionsRevoked })`. This also closes the read-after-write race window. The DB trigger (migration 0003) remains the authoritative last-admin guard regardless.

findings.title: sign-in success path issues 4 sequential DB RTTs that could be partially parallelised
findings.evidence: src/modules/auth/application/sign-in.ts:350-364 — on the success path, after argon2id passes, the following are issued sequentially: (1) `deps.users.clearFailedCount` line 350, (2) `deps.sessions.create` line 351, (3) `deps.users.updateLastSignIn` line 356, (4) `deps.audit.append` line 357. Steps (3) and (4) have no data dependency on each other (both only need `user.id` and `now`, which are already known). Step (2) `sessions.create` is also independent of (1) `clearFailedCount`. Estimated cost: 4 × ~5ms Neon RTT = ~20ms avoidable latency on every successful sign-in.
findings.recommendation: In Application layer (`sign-in.ts`): wrap steps (1)+(2) in a `Promise.all` if they do not share a transaction boundary, then wrap (3)+(4) in a second `Promise.all`. Specifically: `await Promise.all([deps.users.clearFailedCount(user.id), deps.sessions.create({...})])` followed by destructuring; then `await Promise.all([deps.users.updateLastSignIn(user.id, now), deps.audit.append({...})])`. Note: `sessions.create` must resolve before the session object can be returned to the caller, so it cannot be deferred. This saves approximately 2 sequential RTTs per successful sign-in.

findings.title: Proxy path-matching for F9 admin dashboard routes is absent — no kill-switch guard in proxy.ts
findings.evidence: src/proxy.ts blocks F3 (/api/portal, /api/members), F4 (/api/invoices, /api/credit-notes), F5, F7, F8 paths via kill-switch env-var guards (lines 256-397). There is no guard for F9 paths (`/api/cron/insights/**`, `/api/internal/exports/**`, `/api/portal/account/data-export/**`, `/api/portal/directory/**`). The F9 feature is on branch `015-admin-dashboard` (confirmed by CLAUDE.md + git status showing modified F9 files). Without a `FEATURE_F9_DASHBOARD` proxy block, the cron process-export-jobs route and download routes are always reachable even if an operator needs to disable F9 post-launch.
findings.recommendation: Add an `isF9Path` block to `src/proxy.ts` (Presentation layer) checking `env.features.f9Dashboard` (add the env var to `src/lib/env.ts` and Vercel env checklists). Cover: `/api/cron/insights/**`, `/api/internal/exports/**`, `/api/portal/account/data-export/**`, `/api/portal/directory/**`, `/admin/audit/**`, `/admin/directory/**`. Return `build503('feature_disabled', ...)`. Default the flag to `true` (F9 ships as part of this branch) or `false` (dark ship) per the ops decision.

findings.title: Argon2id dummy-hash is computed lazily on first cold-start sign-in attempt — adds ~50ms to first cold-start unknown-email path only
findings.evidence: src/modules/auth/infrastructure/password/argon2-hasher.ts:68-75 — `getDummyHash()` uses `let dummyHashPromise: Promise<string> | null = null` and computes the dummy hash on first call. The hash computation uses the same ARGON2_OPTIONS (memory=19456 KiB, t=2) which takes ~50ms. On a Vercel cold-start, the first `signInImpl` call that hits an unknown email address will compute this hash. Subsequent warm calls reuse the cached promise.
findings.recommendation: Consider warming the dummy hash during module initialisation (module load time) rather than lazily. Add a top-level `void getDummyHash()` call after the `Argon2Hasher` class definition (line 144 of argon2-hasher.ts). This pays the ~50ms cost at module import time (cold start) rather than on the first sign-in request. The Infrastructure layer is the correct place for this. The timing-equality invariant then holds from the first request forward. No urgency: the SLO is not breached.

findings.title: FR-025 bounce inline warning: user-facing piece missing from forgot-password-form
findings.evidence: spec.md:519-522 (FR-025): 'If the first email delivery has failed at the provider (detected via the Resend webhook — see contracts/auth-api.md § 12), an operational alert MUST fire to the on-call team and the user MUST see an inline message explaining that there may be a delay and suggesting they check their spam folder or contact support.' src/components/auth/forgot-password-form.tsx (entire file): no polling call, no SSE listener, no delivery-status check — the form only manages countdown/resend logic. src/app/api/webhooks/resend/route.ts:268-277 emits logger.warn (operational alert satisfied) and inserts into email_delivery_events but there is no client-reachable API that exposes bounce status to the waiting form. No delivery-status endpoint exists under src/app/api/auth/. SC-017 second assertion ('The test also verifies that a Resend webhook reporting a bounce triggers a visible inline warning on the waiting page') cannot pass.
findings.recommendation: Add a polling endpoint GET /api/auth/email-delivery-status?requestId={id} that queries `email_delivery_events` by the message_id correlated to the in-flight reset request. The forgot-password-form polls every 5s after submission and shows an inline warn banner if the response contains a bounce event. Alternatively, use a simpler approach: store the Resend message_id returned by the send call in a short-lived Redis key keyed by the rate-limit bucket, then expose it via a lightweight status endpoint. This is competitive (good UX) but not blocking: the basic forgot-password flow (request → email → link → reset) works correctly without it.

findings.title: FR-025 invitation resend affordance: user-initiated resend intentionally absent — design divergence from spec literal
findings.evidence: spec.md:512-514 (FR-025): 'Every email-dependent flow (password reset, invitation, future account-recovery notifications) MUST provide a resend email affordance that the user can invoke after 60 seconds.' src/app/(auth-public)/invite/[token]/page.tsx:28-31 comment: 'no request new link affordance — the admin must explicitly re-invite because invitations are admin-scoped, not user-scoped.' The page renders a link-invalid card with 'contact administrator' copy. SC-017 only tests forgot-password + reset flows; no corresponding SC for invitation resend. The F3 reissue-invitation use case (src/modules/auth/application/reissue-invitation.ts) provides the admin-side resend path.
findings.recommendation: Acknowledge and document the scope divergence: the spec's literal reading ('invitation' flow = user-initiated resend) conflicts with the spec's own trust model (invitations are admin-scoped; a pending invitee cannot generate their own new token). The current implementation is the correct product decision. Add a note in spec.md FR-025 clarifying that 'invitation' resend is admin-initiated via the admin user list, not user-initiated on the /invite/[token] page. No code change required.

findings.title: feeConfigMissing error surfaced to staff UI contains developer-only instruction 'Run the seed script'
findings.evidence:
src/i18n/messages/en.json:807 — "Tenant fee configuration not yet initialised. Run the seed script." Rendered inline at src/app/(staff)/admin/plans/page.tsx:136 inside a

when result.error.type === 'fee_config_missing'. TH at th.json:807: "กรุณารัน seed script". SV at sv.json:807: "Kör seed-skriptet." All three locales retain the developer instruction verbatim. There is no recovery CTA, no link to settings, and no operator-friendly guidance.
findings.recommendation:
Replace with operator-friendly copy that describes the symptom and provides a contact path. Suggested EN: "Membership fee settings have not been configured yet. Please contact your platform administrator to complete setup." | TH: "ยังไม่ได้ตั้งค่าค่าธรรมเนียมสมาชิก กรุณาติดต่อผู้ดูแลระบบเพื่อดำเนินการ" | SV: "Avgiftsinställningarna för medlemskap är inte konfigurerade än. Kontakta din plattformsadministratör för att slutföra konfigurationen." Remove 'Tenant' and 'seed script' from all three locales. Optionally add a mailto/support link as a secondary CTA in the

render.

findings.title: SLUG_PATTERN duplicated in tenant-context.ts and tenant-slug.ts without sharing the exported constant
findings.evidence: src/modules/tenants/domain/tenant-context.ts:46 — `const SLUG_PATTERN = /^[a-z0-9-]{1,63}$/;` (private). src/modules/tenants/domain/tenant-slug.ts:27 — identical `const SLUG_PATTERN = /^[a-z0-9-]{1,63}$/;` (private). The canonical export `TENANT_SLUG_PATTERN` in tenant-context.ts:75 exists but tenant-slug.ts does not import it; instead both modules define the pattern independently. src/lib/db.ts:250 and src/app/api/cron/outbox-dispatch/route.ts:447 also inline the regex rather than importing the exported constant, which is how the P1 off-by-one arose.
findings.recommendation: In tenant-slug.ts, remove the local `SLUG_PATTERN` declaration and import it from tenant-context.ts, or extract the regex into a shared private constant in a third internal file. Additionally, lint against inline tenant slug regexes in production code (ESLint rule `no-restricted-syntax` on the literal pattern) to prevent future divergence.

findings.title: unsafeBrandTenantSlug exported from public barrel with documentation-only call-site enforcement — no ESLint rule blocks new violating call sites
findings.evidence: src/modules/tenants/index.ts:28 — `unsafeBrandTenantSlug` re-exported from the module's public barrel. src/modules/tenants/domain/tenant-slug.ts:60-82 — allowed production call sites are enumerated in a JSDoc comment but there is no `eslint-plugin-no-restricted-imports` rule (or equivalent AST-level gate) that would fail CI if a new production file imports and calls `unsafeBrandTenantSlug` outside the approved set (hmac-signer.ts, tenant-context.ts, member.ts). Verified: grepping `src/` for `unsafeBrandTenantSlug` shows only three production callers, all in the allowed set. However the guard is governance-by-comment only.
findings.recommendation: Add an ESLint `no-restricted-imports` rule (or a dedicated `@/lib/no-unsafe-brand` wrapper) that reports an error if `unsafeBrandTenantSlug` is imported outside the three approved files. Alternatively, move `unsafeBrandTenantSlug` to a separate barrel (`@/modules/tenants/unsafe`) that is explicitly excluded from the `no-restricted-imports` allow-list so violations surface at lint time rather than review time. This is table-stakes governance hygiene for the F10 multi-tenant expansion.

findings.title: No dedicated unit tests for asTenantSlug or unsafeBrandTenantSlug — rejection cases and the weak tryTenantId pattern-gap are untested
findings.evidence: tests/unit/tenants/ contains only tenant-context.test.ts and iana-timezone.test.ts. There is no tenant-slug.test.ts. Verified via Glob: no file matches `tests/unit/tenants/**/*slug*`. `asTenantSlug` (the validated constructor) and `unsafeBrandTenantSlug` (the unsafe escape hatch) have zero dedicated tests. Additionally, tests/unit/members/domain/member-state.test.ts:194-209 tests `tryTenantId` but does NOT assert that e.g. `tryTenantId('SweCham')` returns `ok` (it does — the non-empty check passes uppercase slugs), and no test documents that this is intentional (the JSDoc at tenant-slug.ts:77 notes the gap but there is no test enforcing or documenting the accepted contract).
findings.recommendation: Create `tests/unit/tenants/domain/tenant-slug.test.ts` covering: (a) `asTenantSlug` happy path (valid slug returns branded string), (b) `asTenantSlug` rejection cases matching the existing `asTenantContext` rejection suite (uppercase, spaces, underscore, dot, slash, >63 chars, empty, non-string), (c) a documentation test that `tryTenantId('SweCham').ok === true` with an explicit comment explaining why the relaxed check is intentional (DB-read hydration path where upstream RLS already gated the value). This is TDD discipline per Constitution Principle II.

findings.title: Missing unit tests for tenant-slug.ts — asTenantSlug and unsafeBrandTenantSlug have no dedicated test coverage
findings.evidence: tests/unit/tenants/ contains only domain/tenant-context.test.ts and iana-timezone.test.ts. No test file exists for src/modules/tenants/domain/tenant-slug.ts. asTenantSlug (the validated brand constructor, exported at index.ts:27) and unsafeBrandTenantSlug (security-sensitive escape hatch with enumerated call-site restrictions documented in tenant-slug.ts:59-82) have zero direct unit tests. The asTenantContext tests provide indirect coverage via the internal unsafeBrandTenantSlug call, but the separate InvalidTenantSlugStringError error class (thrown by asTenantSlug, not by asTenantContext) is never exercised. Constitution Principle II requires 100% Domain line coverage.
findings.recommendation: Add tests/unit/tenants/tenant-slug.test.ts covering: (a) asTenantSlug happy paths (valid slugs), (b) asTenantSlug rejection paths (empty string, uppercase, underscores, 64-char, non-string inputs at runtime), (c) InvalidTenantSlugStringError carries the attempted slug, (d) unsafeBrandTenantSlug returns branded value without validation. Mirror the structure of the existing tenant-context.test.ts.

findings.title: Missing architecture scan test backstop for the tenants barrel — ESLint no-restricted-imports rule is silently shadowed
findings.evidence: eslint.config.mjs:294-303 defines a no-restricted-imports pattern blocking @/modules/tenants/domain/** deep imports from outside the module. However, the known ESLint flat-config last-wins shadow bug (documented in tests/unit/architecture/broadcasts-barrel.test.ts:10-20 and specs/014-email-broadcast-advance/retrospective.md) means the events block at eslint.config.mjs:692 (files: src/**/*.{ts,tsx}) silently overwrites the tenants barrel rule for all src/ files. The broadcasts, events, and insights modules each have a source-scan architecture test as a backstop (tests/unit/architecture/broadcasts-barrel.test.ts, events-barrel.test.ts, insights-barrel.test.ts). No equivalent test exists for src/modules/tenants. The tenants barrel is currently clean — 4 src/modules/auth/application and src/modules/members/application files do import @/modules/tenants/domain/tenant-slug directly, but these are within their own modules which are legitimately excluded from the barrel guard (eslint.config.mjs:219-226 ignores).
findings.recommendation: Add tests/unit/architecture/tenants-barrel.test.ts scanning src/app/** and src/components/** for deep imports into @/modules/tenants/(domain|application|infrastructure)/. Model after the insights-barrel.test.ts (KNOWN_BACKLOG starts empty — baseline is clean). Scan patterns: /from\s+['"]@\/modules\/tenants\/(domain)\//, /from\s+['"]\.\.?.*\/modules\/tenants\/domain\//.

findings.title: Plan detail page renders benefit-matrix enum values in English for TH/SV locales
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/plans/[year]/[planId]/page.tsx:224-291 + 302-313 — benefit enum values (website_page_type, homepage_logo_category, directory_listing_size, event_discount_scope, directory_ad_position) are passed to the local KV component WITHOUT the `raw` flag, so they flow through `humanize()` (line 302: snake_case → English Title Case). Confirmed translations already exist: th.json:700-733 has admin.plans.create.options.* keys (member_news_update='ข่าวสมาชิก', all_employees='พนักงานทั้งหมด', first_10_pages='10 หน้าแรก', etc.). The page already imports `tOptions = getTranslations('admin.plans.create.options')` at line 68 but never uses it for these values — the editor (benefit-matrix-editor.tsx:129-171) localizes the same enums correctly, only the read/detail view regressed.
findings.recommendation: Replace `humanize(value)` for the enum fields with the matching `tOptions(...)` lookups, e.g. value={tOptions(`websitePageType.${plan.benefit_matrix.website_page_type ?? 'none'}`)}, value={tOptions(`eventDiscountScope.${...}`)}, value={tOptions(`directoryAdPosition.${...}`)}, etc. Keep `raw` numeric fields (eblast_per_year, cultural_tickets) as-is. After the swap, the local `humanize()` helper can likely be deleted (verify no remaining caller).

findings.title: NumberField <Label> not linked to <Input> via htmlFor — WCAG SC 1.3.1 / SC 4.1.2
findings.evidence: src/components/plans/benefit-matrix-editor.tsx:71-88 — useId() generates an id that is set on <Input id={id}> (line 76) but <Label> on line 74 has no htmlFor={id}. Affects every numeric benefit field rendered by BenefitMatrixEditor (eblast_per_year, cultural_tickets_per_year, event_tickets_included, website_logo_months, banner_per_year) in both the Create wizard (plan-form-wizard.tsx) and the Edit flat form (plan-edit-form.tsx). Screen readers do not associate the label with the input; clicking the label does not focus the field.
findings.recommendation: Change line 74 from `<Label>{label}</Label>` to `<Label htmlFor={id}>{label}</Label>`. The id is already generated and assigned to the Input — the Label just needs to consume it.

findings.title: Plan detail page renders enum values via hardcoded humanize() — breaks TH/SV locale
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/plans/[year]/[planId]/page.tsx:227,231,235,245,290 — Five enum fields (website_page_type, homepage_logo_category, directory_listing_size, event_discount_scope, directory_ad_position) are passed to the KV component without raw=true, so they go through humanize() which does an underscore-to-space + Title Case transform. All locale files already have the translated option strings under admin.plans.create.options.*. The tOptions translation function is imported at line 68 but never used in the KV calls.
findings.recommendation: Replace humanize(value) calls with tOptions(key) lookups matching the same pattern used in PlansTable (plans-table.tsx:421). For nullable fields add a null-guard fallback to '—'. Example for website_page_type: `value={plan.benefit_matrix.website_page_type ? tOptions('websitePageType.' + plan.benefit_matrix.website_page_type.replace(/-/g,'_')) : '—'}` with raw prop set.

findings.title: softDeletePlan: TOCTOU window between countActivePlanMembers and planRepo.softDelete
findings.evidence: src/modules/plans/application/soft-delete-plan.ts:87-123 — step 3 calls `deps.members.countActivePlanMembers(deps.tenant, input.planId, input.year)` in one DB round-trip, returns to the Application layer, then step 4 calls `deps.planRepo.softDelete(...)` in a separate DB round-trip. Between these two calls, a concurrent mutation (member plan assignment, or a racing `changePlan` use-case in F3) could move a member from another plan onto this plan, raising the live-member count from 0 to 1. The soft-delete then succeeds, leaving active members referencing a soft-deleted plan. There is no advisory lock, SELECT FOR UPDATE, or DB-level FK blocking this window. The `drizzle-member-attachment-checker` (`src/modules/plans/infrastructure/members/drizzle-member-attachment-checker.ts`) delegates to F3's `countActiveMembersOnPlan`, which runs its own `runInTenant` without any locking.
findings.recommendation: Wrap both the countActivePlanMembers check and the softDelete into a single `runInTenant` transaction with a `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` lock on the plan row before counting. Alternative: acquire a per-(tenant, planId, year) advisory lock (`pg_advisory_xact_lock`) in the repo's `softDelete` method, and re-check the count inside the same transaction. The advisory lock namespace should be distinct from F4 `invoicing:`, F5 `payments:`, F7 `broadcasts:` — use `plans:soft-delete:${tenantSlug}:${planId}:${year}`.

findings.title: planPatchSchema comment misrepresents merged-result validation (misleading for future maintainers)
findings.evidence: src/modules/plans/domain/plan-validators.ts:220-224 — the comment reads 'When only one side is patched, the merged-result validation in update-plan.ts catches cross-field mismatches against the full plan.' No such merged-result validation exists in update-plan.ts. The update-plan.ts flow (lines 147-262) loads the plan, validates the patch with `planPatchSchema.safeParse`, checks locked fields, and calls `planRepo.update` — there is no step that merges the existing plan with the patch and re-runs `planSchema.safeParse`. The comment was written to describe an intended-but-unimplemented defence, which is now the documented rationale for skipping a more thorough check in the zod superRefine.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) delete or correct the comment to accurately describe what the code does, or (b) implement the merged-result validation the comment promises and add a test for the `plan_category`-only patch scenario. If the patch is implemented as part of the P1 fix, the comment can be updated to describe the new merged-result check.

findings.title: cross-tenant probe test not verifiable in-module (Principle I sub-clause 3)
findings.evidence: src/modules/plans/infrastructure/db/plan-repo.ts:122-159 (findByTenantAndYear), src/modules/plans/infrastructure/db/plan-repo.ts:162-177 (findOne). Both methods delegate tenant isolation entirely to RLS via runInTenant. The cross-tenant probe integration test for the membership_plans surface is seeded in tests/unit/insights/set-directory-logo.test.ts per stage0 baseline but the dedicated plans cross-tenant probe test (stage0 baseline B0-I4: us3-tenant-isolation.test.ts) has a seed bug (camelCase memberId vs snake_case member_id) that makes the probe assertion non-evaluable in isolation, even though the product code is correct.
findings.recommendation: Fix the test seed in tests/integration/plans/us3-tenant-isolation.test.ts: change `payload: { memberId: … }` to `payload: { member_id: … }` to match the snake_case key the production emitter (change-plan.ts:244) writes. Confirm the test passes in isolation before the go-live gate. This is a one-line seed fix, not a code fix.

findings.title: plan-audit-adapter logger.error passes raw err string — verify forbidden-field exclusion at Infrastructure boundary
findings.evidence: src/modules/plans/infrastructure/audit/plan-audit-adapter.ts:121-136. The catch block passes `err: message` (a string extracted from `e.message`) to logger.error. If a Postgres error message includes connection-string fragments (e.g., DSN password in misconfigured environments), the string lands in structured logs.
findings.recommendation: No code change required for launch. For post-launch hardening: consider adding a pino redact rule for /err.message/ patterns matching connection-string keywords (`postgresql://`, `@neon.tech`) in `src/lib/logger.ts`, mirroring the existing forbidden-field redact list.

findings.title: searchPlans palette route passes raw caught exception objects to logger.warn — potential structured log noise from sub-query errors
findings.evidence: src/app/api/plans/search/route.ts:113-120 (members sub-query catch), lines 252-261 (refundable invoices outer catch). Both pass `err: e` (the raw caught value, which may be an Error object) directly to logger.warn/error. The pino logger serialises Error objects including .stack. Stack traces can expose internal file paths and line numbers.
findings.recommendation: No code change required for launch. Post-launch: normalise catch blocks to `err: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)` to avoid stack-trace fan-out in Vercel log drain, matching the pattern already used in plan-audit-adapter.ts.

findings.title: Bare ⚠ glyph used for missing-translation badge instead of a lucide icon
findings.evidence: src/components/plans/locale-text-display.tsx:55 ({locale.toUpperCase()} ⚠) and src/components/plans/locale-text-input.tsx:98 ({l.label} ⚠) append a literal U+26A0 emoji glyph into the badge text via a JS string.
findings.recommendation: Swap the ⚠ glyph for a sized lucide icon (e.g. <AlertTriangle className="size-3" aria-hidden />) inside the Badge, keeping the existing title attribute for the SR/hover hint. Apply in both files for consistency.

findings.title: Prior-year lock banner hand-rolls an outline-button className instead of reusing buttonVariants
findings.evidence: src/components/plans/prior-year-lock-banner.tsx:38-44 — the 'Clone to current year' link uses a manual className: 'inline-flex items-center rounded-md border border-input bg-background px-3 py-1.5 text-sm font-medium shadow-sm hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground'. The list page (page.tsx:59) and the empty-state CTAs (plans-table.tsx:371) both correctly use buttonVariants({ variant: 'outline' }).
findings.recommendation: Replace the manual className with className={buttonVariants({ variant: 'outline', size: 'sm' })} on the <Link>, matching the empty-state and page-header CTA pattern already used elsewhere in this module.

findings.title: Plans <Table> missing <TableCaption> — WCAG SC 1.3.1
findings.evidence: src/components/plans/plans-table.tsx — The <Table> element (around line 323) has no <TableCaption> and no aria-label/aria-labelledby on the table element itself. All column heads carry scope='col' (correct) but the table itself has no programmatic label. Compare: other tables in the codebase (audit-log, members directory) include a <TableCaption className='sr-only'>.
findings.recommendation: Add `<TableCaption className='sr-only'>{t('title')} {year}</TableCaption>` as the first child of <Table>. The i18n key admin.plans.title already exists in all three locales. This provides a screen-reader-only caption without visual change.

findings.title: Wizard Save button missing Loader2 spinner and aria-busy during submission
findings.evidence: src/components/plans/plan-form-wizard.tsx:538-544 — The Save button on the Review step changes label text to tButtons('saving') during submitting but renders no <Loader2Icon> and no aria-busy attribute. Contrast with PlanEditForm save button at src/components/plans/plan-edit-form.tsx:274 which has both: `<Loader2Icon className='mr-2 h-4 w-4 motion-safe:animate-spin' aria-hidden />` and `aria-busy={submitting}`.
findings.recommendation: Import Loader2Icon from lucide-react and mirror the edit-form pattern: `<Button type='button' onClick={handleSubmit} disabled={!stepValid.review || submitting} aria-busy={submitting}>{submitting && <Loader2Icon className='mr-2 h-4 w-4 motion-safe:animate-spin' aria-hidden />}{submitting ? tButtons('saving') : tButtons('save')}</Button>`.

findings.title: read_only_mode 503 response falls through to generic toast in all mutation clients
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/plans/new/new-plan-client.tsx:53-57, src/app/(staff)/admin/plans/[year]/[planId]/edit/edit-plan-client.tsx:92-99, src/app/(staff)/admin/plans/clone/clone-year-client.tsx:71-77, src/components/plans/plans-table.tsx:165-187 — When READ_ONLY_MODE=true, the API returns {error:{code:'read_only_mode'}} with HTTP 503 (src/app/api/plans/_read-only-guard.ts:41-51). None of the four mutation clients check for this error code; they fall through to t('errors.generic'). The translated key errors.readOnlyMode ('The system is currently in read-only mode for maintenance.') exists in all three locale files (en.json:196, th.json, sv.json) but is never consumed.
findings.recommendation: In each of the four client submit handlers, add a branch before the generic fallback: `else if (errorCode === 'read_only_mode') { toast.error(tErrors('readOnlyMode')); }` where tErrors reads from the global 'errors' namespace. The key already exists; this is a one-line addition per client.

findings.title: JSONB ilike triple-OR on plan_name has no GIN expression index
findings.evidence: src/modules/plans/infrastructure/db/plan-repo.ts:143-146: findByTenantAndYear uses ilike(sql`${membershipPlans.planName}->>'en'`, ...) OR ilike(...->>'th'...) OR ilike(...->>'sv'...) when filter.q is set. Grep for 'gin|GIN|pg_trgm|plan_name.*idx|CREATE.*INDEX.*plan_name' across all drizzle/migrations/*.sql returns zero matches. No expression index exists on the extracted JSONB text fields.
findings.recommendation: Post-launch: if GET /api/plans?q= becomes a performance concern, add a GEN_RANDOM_UUID()-stable expression index: CREATE INDEX membership_plans_plan_name_en_trgm_idx ON membership_plans USING gin (lower((plan_name->>'en')) gin_trgm_ops) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL; (and th/sv variants). Alternatively, consider consolidating the free-text search into the existing in-memory path (searchPlans already does this correctly) and removing the q-passthrough in findByTenantAndYear to eliminate the ambiguity.

findings.title: GET /api/plans incurs uncached cross-module RTT for taxPolicy on every request
findings.evidence: src/modules/plans/application/list-plans.ts:105 calls deps.taxPolicy() on every invocation of listPlans. plans-deps.ts:57-66 wires taxPolicy as async () => getTenantTaxPolicy(makeGetTenantTaxPolicyDeps(), tenant.slug) — a live Neon SELECT on tenant_invoice_settings with no per-request or per-tenant caching. Every GET /api/plans = 2 Neon RTTs (taxPolicy SELECT + plans SELECT). taxPolicy data (VAT rate, currency) changes at most annually.
findings.recommendation: Post-launch: add a request-scoped or short-TTL (60s) cache for taxPolicy in plans-deps.ts buildPlansDeps. The Application layer already treats taxPolicy as a thunk so no interface changes are needed — only the Infrastructure wiring changes. Ensure the cache key is tenant.slug-scoped to preserve isolation.

findings.title: renewal_tier_bucket column on membership_plans is not surfaced in Plan domain type — silent mismatch between DB row and domain object
findings.evidence: src/modules/plans/infrastructure/db/schema.ts line 143: `renewalTierBucket: text('renewal_tier_bucket').notNull().default('regular')`. src/modules/plans/infrastructure/db/plan-repo.ts lines 91-116 (rowToPlan): the field is never mapped — it is silently dropped when a DB row is translated to the Plan domain object. src/modules/plans/domain/plan.ts lines 85-122: Plan type has no renewal_tier_bucket field. Downstream F8 reads renewal_tier_bucket by JOIN-querying membershipPlans directly (src/modules/renewals/infrastructure/drizzle/drizzle-plan-catalog.ts) bypassing rowToPlan, so F8 works today. However, any future Application-layer code that wants to inspect this field on a Plan object cannot do so without reaching through Infrastructure.
findings.recommendation: Add renewal_tier_bucket: string (or a typed union matching the DB CHECK values) to the Plan domain type and map it in rowToPlan. F8 Infrastructure adapters that already JOIN the column directly can be refactored to use the domain field over time. Scope is additive — no existing callers break.

findings.title: cancelScheduledPlanChange use-case is exported and has a live route but no admin UI wires it — dead API surface at launch
findings.evidence: src/modules/plans/index.ts lines 310-312 exports cancelScheduledPlanChange. src/app/api/admin/scheduled-plan-changes/[id]/cancel/route.ts is fully implemented (POST, RBAC, idempotency, audit). src/modules/plans/application/cancel-scheduled-plan-change.ts line 17 comment says 'Caller: admin API route at src/app/api/admin/scheduled-plan-changes/[id]/cancel/route.ts'. No admin page or component in src/app/(staff)/admin imports or calls this route. grep for 'scheduled-plan-changes' in src/app/(staff) returns zero results.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) add a 'Cancel scheduled change' button to the renewal cycle detail page (/admin/renewals/[cycleId]) — the page already has fetchers that pull cycle data — or (b) document the API-only status explicitly in the F8 spec/tasks and schedule the UI for a post-launch sprint. Do NOT remove the backend — it is correct and tested.

findings.title: fee_config_updated audit event type is retired but still in F1 pgEnum — stale enum value in production schema
findings.evidence: src/modules/plans/domain/audit-event.ts lines 51-57: F2_AUDIT_EVENT_TYPES does NOT include 'fee_config_updated'. Comment on line 43 says: 'fee_config_updated was retired in R7/R8 (migration 0029); the pgEnum value remains in F1 schema for historical rows but is NOT in this Domain union.' src/modules/plans/application/ports.ts lines 158-163 comment confirms FeeConfigRepo was removed. src/modules/plans/infrastructure/db/schema.ts lines 181-185 confirms tenant_fee_config table was DROPPED. The pgEnum value 'fee_config_updated' therefore lives in the Postgres audit_event_type enum serving no new rows but blocking enum cleanup.
findings.recommendation: Add an additive migration to REMOVE the 'fee_config_updated' value from the audit_event_type pgEnum IF zero historical rows use it (verify with SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE event_type='fee_config_updated'). If rows exist, leave the pgEnum value intact and add a DB comment documenting it as retired. Either way, add a comment to schema.ts or the migration file explaining the decision.

findings.title: deferred-to-f3.md D1 'Resolved' section still empty despite F3 shipping — maintenance artifact misleads future readers
findings.evidence: specs/002-membership-plans/deferred-to-f3.md lines 88-89: '## Resolved in F3 — None yet — F3 hasn't shipped.' F3 shipped per CLAUDE.md ('F3 Members & Contacts ... SHIPPED'). The BundleChangeWarningDialog component (T095) was built. The file's invariant clause (line 16-19) states it MUST NOT be deleted until every row is resolved or rejected — but the Resolved section was never updated after F3 shipped.
findings.recommendation: After wiring the bundle-change warning (P1 finding above), move D1 to the Resolved section with a pointer to the commit. If the wiring is deferred to post-launch, add a note 'Partially delivered — component T095 exists, client wiring pending, tracked as [issue/task].'

findings.title: Zod validation messages in MemberForm are hardcoded English strings, not i18n keys
findings.evidence: src/components/members/member-form.tsx:51 — `z.string().trim().min(1, 'required')` (company_name); :55 — `.length(2, 'Use ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code, e.g. TH, SE, US')` (country); :73 — `z.string().min(1, 'required')` (plan_id); :90–92 — `min(1, 'required')` on first_name, last_name, email. These literal strings are rendered raw via FieldError (line 163) — TH/SV locale users see English error copy.
findings.recommendation: Replace the hardcoded string arguments with translated strings resolved via `useTranslations`. Pattern: call `const tf = useTranslations('admin.members.create.fields')` at the top of the component, then build the schema inside the component body using `tf('fieldRequired')` and `tf('countryFormat')`. Add the `fieldRequired` and `countryFormat` keys to all three locale files (EN/TH/SV). The `phoneError` field (line 106) already follows the correct i18n pattern — mirror that. `contactForm.fieldRequired` in the same codebase is also a working reference.

findings.title: InlineCountryCell and InlineNotesCell enter-edit-mode via onDoubleClick only — keyboard users cannot activate inline edit (WCAG 2.1 SC 2.1.1)
findings.evidence: src/components/members/members-table.tsx:386 — `<button ... onDoubleClick={startEdit}>` (InlineCountryCell); :500 — `<button ... onDoubleClick={startEdit}>` (InlineNotesCell). Neither has `onClick`. Pressing Enter or Space on a focused `<button>` fires a synthetic `click` event, not `dblclick`. The `startEdit` callback is therefore unreachable by keyboard. InlineStatusCell (line 302) correctly uses `onClick` and is not affected.
findings.recommendation: Add `onClick={startEdit}` alongside `onDoubleClick={startEdit}` on both button elements. Mouse users who single-click will enter edit mode (acceptable — the edit field auto-selects and Escape cancels cleanly). Double-click remains as a convenience gesture. If the product decision is that single-click must not trigger edit (e.g. because single-click might be used for row selection in future), add `onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); startEdit(); } }}` instead. Apply the same fix to `useInlineEditField` documentation so future callers know the keyboard entry contract.

findings.title: FR-009a: tax_id not enforced as required for Corporate/Partnership plan tiers
findings.evidence: src/modules/members/application/use-cases/create-member.ts:53 — `tax_id: z.string().max(50).nullable().optional()` with no plan-category check. src/modules/members/application/use-cases/update-member.ts:37 — identical nullable/optional. src/modules/members/application/ports/plan-lookup-port.ts:23 — `PlanSummary.planCategory` is available but neither use case reads it to require the field. The `asTaxId` value-object validates format only when a non-null value is supplied; it is never called when the field is omitted on a Corporate or Partnership member.
findings.recommendation: In `create-member` and `update-member`, after resolving the plan via `PlanLookupPort.getPlan`, check `plan.planCategory`: if `'corporate'` or `'partnership'` and `tax_id` is null/absent, return a typed error `{ type: 'tax_id_required' }` (or include in `invalid_body`). Add the `requiresTaxId` boolean to `PlanSummary` port (derivable from `planCategory !== null`) to keep the enforcement point in Application without hard-coding the tier list. Add a unit test asserting create-member rejects a Premium Corporate member submitted without a tax_id.

findings.title: Bulk `send_portal_invite` is audit-only stub — no invitations are actually dispatched
findings.evidence: src/modules/members/application/use-cases/bulk-action.ts:306-334 — the `send_portal_invite` case writes only a `member_portal_invite_queued` audit row and explicitly notes 'invite dispatch deferred — audit only'. `updatedCount` is NOT incremented. The individual `invitePortal` use-case (src/modules/members/application/use-cases/invite-portal.ts) is complete and wired for single-contact invites. The bulk UI button (`src/app/(staff)/admin/members/_components/bulk-action-bar.tsx:175,212`) is fully visible to admins with no 'coming soon' indicator.
findings.recommendation: Wire `invitePortal` (or the lighter `inviteUserForMember`) inside the `send_portal_invite` branch of `bulk-action.ts`, iterating over each member's primary contact. Post-commit fan-out (outside the main transaction) is acceptable per the single-invite pattern — the primary concern is that the outbox row is enqueued. Skip already-linked contacts (return `already_linked` sub-error per member, not a batch abort). Update `updatedCount` to reflect members where an invite was successfully queued. Add an integration test asserting that a bulk send_portal_invite results in outbox rows.

findings.title: member-self-update: pre-tx ownership reads are not re-validated inside the transaction
findings.evidence: src/modules/members/application/use-cases/member-self-update.ts:194–211 — memberRepo.findById and contactRepo.findById run outside runInTenant. The tx at line 287 calls updateFieldsInTx / updateInTx using the stale patch built from those reads. A concurrent archive between the pre-tx read and the tx means a member that is now archived gets a website/description update written without the archived guard that inline-edit enforces (inline-edit.ts:190–193 checks status inside the FOR UPDATE block).
findings.recommendation: Inside the runInTenant block, re-read the member with findByIdInTx (FOR UPDATE) and assert status !== 'archived' before writing. Matches the inline-edit pattern exactly.

findings.title: mark-invitation-bounced leaks raw repo error code in server_error message
findings.evidence: src/modules/members/application/use-cases/mark-invitation-bounced.ts:96 — catch block: return err({ type: 'server_error', message: re.code }) passes the raw repo error code string ('repo.unexpected') as the public-facing message. Consistent with the project convention (inline-edit, archive-member, etc.) that server_error messages should be sanitized.
findings.recommendation: Change to return err({ type: 'server_error', message: 'mark-invitation-bounced: unexpected' }) and log re.code via logger.error({ cause: re.code }) before the return.

findings.title: Portal profile GET exposes company tax_id and turnover_thb to portal member sessions
findings.evidence: src/app/api/portal/profile/route.ts:21-54 — local serialiseMember() explicitly redacts notes (line 52) but includes tax_id (implicitly, field is present on the member object) and turnover_thb. The function is defined locally at lines 21-54 and exposes the same fields as the admin serializer for those columns. Line 120-125: `NextResponse.json({ ...serialiseMember(result.value.member), contacts: ... })` — the tax_id and turnover_thb fields are present in result.value.member (populated by drizzle-member-repo rowToMember) and forwarded to the portal user.
findings.recommendation: Remove tax_id and turnover_thb from the portal's local serialiseMember() (src/app/api/portal/profile/route.ts lines 21-54). Add a contract test asserting GET /api/portal/profile does not include tax_id or turnover_thb. Test ID: CHK-F3-02.

findings.title: listLinkedUserIdsForMemberInTx silently returns [] on DB error, archive cascade may proceed without full session revocation
findings.evidence: src/modules/members/infrastructure/db/drizzle-contact-repo.ts:207-236 — on any DB exception the function catches, logs via logger.error, and returns [] rather than propagating the error. archive-member.ts:151-154 uses this return value to build uniqueLinkedUserIds; if it silently returns [] the archive proceeds, status is committed, but NO sessions are revoked and the former portal user retains an active session post-archive.
findings.recommendation: When listLinkedUserIdsForMemberInTx returns an empty array due to error (distinguish via a returned boolean flag or typed error result), emit a `member_archive_session_revocation_skipped` audit event inside the archive tx with `payload: { member_id, reason: 'contact_lookup_failed', error_class }`. This creates an audit breadcrumb that monitoring can alert on, and gives operators a clear remediation signal to manually revoke sessions. Test ID: CHK-F3-03.

findings.title: Overdue invoice-amount cell hardcodes amber instead of the warning token
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/members/[memberId]/_components/member-invoices-section.tsx:365 — 'text-amber-700 dark:text-amber-400' on the remaining-balance cell for unpaid invoices. The inline comment documents a manual 4.8:1 contrast check, i.e. the author re-derived by hand what the --warning token (globals.css:173, L lowered 0.55→0.48 specifically so warning text meets WCAG AA on warning-surface) already guarantees.
findings.recommendation: Use text-warning (the token resolves to the same audited amber and carries its own dark value), dropping the hand-paired dark:text-amber-400 and the manual-contrast comment.

findings.title: MemberPicker status dot hardcodes bg-amber-500 with no dark variant; should use the StatusDot primitive
findings.evidence: src/components/members/member-picker.tsx:97 — STATUS_DOT_CLASS.inactive = 'bg-amber-500' (rendered at line 389 as a size-1.5 / 6px disc). Single fixed value, NO dark: variant (the other entries use bg-muted-foreground which is a token). The codebase ships src/components/ui/status-dot.tsx — documented as the canonical '8px semantic disc for dense surfaces' fed by the same --warning/--success tokens — which is precisely this use case.
findings.recommendation: Replace the bg-amber-500 dot with the StatusDot primitive (tone warning for inactive) so it inherits the audited token + dark value, or at minimum swap to the bg-warning token to gain the dark variant.

findings.title: Members empty/error-state headings stack .text-h3 token utility with conflicting text-lg font-semibold
findings.evidence: src/components/members/empty-states.tsx:41,71,103 — <h2 className="text-h3 text-lg font-semibold">. .text-h3 (globals.css:350-353) sets font-size: var(--font-size-h3) and font-weight: var(--font-weight-heading); the same element then applies Tailwind text-lg (1.125rem) and font-semibold (600), which override the token values via utility source-order. Grep confirms NO other component in src/components composes .text-h1/.text-h2/.text-h3/.text-h4 — these three sites are the only ones, and they are the only ones that bolt extra size/weight utilities onto the token class.
findings.recommendation: Pick one source of truth: either keep className="text-h3" alone (recommended — matches the token system) or drop text-h3 and keep text-lg font-semibold. Do not stack both.

findings.title: Select-all checkbox shows no indeterminate state when rows are partially selected
findings.evidence: src/components/members/members-table.tsx:575 — `checked={table.getIsAllPageRowsSelected()}`. When 1–49 of 50 rows are selected the checkbox renders as unchecked (false), not indeterminate. `table.getIsSomePageRowsSelected()` exists in TanStack Table v8 but is not used.
findings.recommendation: Change line 575 to: `checked={table.getIsSomePageRowsSelected() && !table.getIsAllPageRowsSelected() ? 'indeterminate' : table.getIsAllPageRowsSelected()}`. The shadcn `Checkbox` component already supports `checked='indeterminate'` via Radix `CheckboxPrimitive`. No additional CSS keyframe is needed — Radix renders the dash indicator via its own data attribute.

findings.title: Expired-undelete tooltip in ArchivedBanner is keyboard-inaccessible — inner span has no tabIndex
findings.evidence: src/components/members/archived-banner.tsx:143–150 — `<TooltipTrigger className='inline-flex'><span aria-disabled='true' ...>`. The `<span>` has no `tabIndex` attribute and is not a focusable element. `TooltipTrigger` wraps a non-interactive span, so keyboard focus cannot reach the trigger. The tooltip content (explaining why Undelete is permanently disabled after 90 days) is therefore inaccessible to keyboard-only and screen-reader users.
findings.recommendation: Replace the `<span>` with a `<button type='button' disabled>` or add `tabIndex={0}` to the span. The simplest fix: render a real disabled `<Button>` element (`disabled` + `aria-disabled='true'`) as the TooltipTrigger child. A disabled button is focusable (tabIndex=0 by default in most browsers), so keyboard users can focus it and the tooltip fires. Alternatively, append the tooltip text as an `<span className='sr-only'>` sibling of the button so screen readers read it without requiring focus-triggered tooltip reveal.

findings.title: Missing route-level error.tsx for /admin/members/ and /admin/members/new/ — failures bubble to layout-blowing /admin/error.tsx
findings.evidence: `find src/app/(staff)/admin/members -name 'error.tsx'` returns only `[memberId]/error.tsx`. The directory list (`/admin/members/page.tsx`) and the create page (`/admin/members/new/page.tsx`) have no local error boundary. If `requireSession`, `resolveTenantFromRequest`, or `listPlans` throws, the error propagates to `/admin/error.tsx` (confirmed present at `src/app/(staff)/admin/error.tsx`), which renders without the TableContainer/FormContainer wrapper and wipes sidebar + breadcrumb context for the admin.
findings.recommendation: Create `src/app/(staff)/admin/members/error.tsx` (mirroring `src/app/(staff)/admin/members/[memberId]/error.tsx` — copy the same structure using TableContainer + PageHeader + Card). Create `src/app/(staff)/admin/members/new/error.tsx` with FormContainer framing. Both files can be thin wrappers around the shared `errors.generic` and `buttons.retry` i18n keys already in use.

findings.title: Bulk 'Send portal invite' ConfirmationDialog shows no in-flight loading indicator while request is pending
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/members/_components/bulk-action-bar.tsx:204–213 — `ConfirmationDialog` for invite uses `confirmDisabled={executing}` but has no spinner prop. Compare: `ArchiveConfirmDialog` at line 195–201 receives `pending={executing}` and renders a `Loader2Icon` + disabled state during the in-flight request.
findings.recommendation: `ConfirmationDialog` already accepts `confirmDisabled`. Extend it with an optional `pending?: boolean` prop that renders a `Loader2Icon` alongside the confirm label when true (identical pattern to `ArchiveConfirmDialog`). Pass `pending={executing}` at line 210. Alternatively, use `ArchiveConfirmDialog` as a model and give `ConfirmationDialog` parity on this one prop. If the ConfirmationDialog refactor is out of scope, a quick fix is to replace the invite dialog with a bespoke component (copy ArchiveConfirmDialog, strip the typed-phrase section).

findings.title: Bulk-archive typed-phrase in TH locale requires typing complex Thai script — may block staff without Thai IME active
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/members/_components/archive-confirm-dialog.tsx:59 — `const expectedPhrase = t('archivePhrase', { count })`. TH locale resolves to `'ย้าย {count} สมาชิก ไปถังเก็บ'` (confirmed in `src/i18n/messages/th.json`). Admin staff operating the system in TH locale must type this Thai phrase exactly, which requires their keyboard/IME to be in Thai input mode.
findings.recommendation: Consider offering an invariant English phrase ('Archive N members') regardless of locale for the typed-match confirmation, matching GitHub and Stripe's pattern (they always use English for irreversible-action confirmations). Alternatively, document the IME requirement in the confirmation label hint text (`admin.members.bulk.typeToConfirm`). This is a conscious design choice — if the product decision is that typed confirmation should match the UI locale, add a hint like `t('typeToConfirmIMENote')` for TH locale only to prompt the admin to switch input method.

findings.title: directoryQFilter EXISTS subquery searches contacts.email with ILIKE '%q%' but no trgm GIN index on email — sequential scan inside directory search
findings.evidence: src/modules/members/infrastructure/db/drizzle-member-repo.ts:160-167 — directoryQFilter generates: `c.email ILIKE ${like}` inside an EXISTS correlated subquery. drizzle/migrations/0009_members_contacts.sql:101-103 creates `contacts_name_trgm_gin` on `(first_name || ' ' || last_name)` but no trgm index on `contacts.email`. No subsequent migration adds one (confirmed by grep across all 0006-0199 migrations). SLO: docs/observability.md §14.2 SC-002 substring search p95 < 500ms @ 5k rows. Measurement: UNVERIFIED (no EXPLAIN ANALYZE run available this session).
findings.recommendation:
Infrastructure layer fix: add a trgm GIN index on contacts.email in a new migration:
```sql
CREATE INDEX contacts_email_trgm_gin
  ON contacts USING GIN (email gin_trgm_ops)
  WHERE removed_at IS NULL;
```
This is consistent with the existing contacts_name_trgm_gin pattern in migration 0009. Verify index is used with: `EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) SELECT 1 FROM contacts c WHERE c.tenant_id = '<slug>' AND c.member_id = '<uuid>' AND c.removed_at IS NULL AND c.email ILIKE '%test@%';` — expect Bitmap Index Scan on contacts_email_trgm_gin.

findings.title: findLastPlanChangedAt uses raw sql`...` template that bypasses runInTenant RLS guard on audit_log — implicit tenant_id filter in query instead of SET LOCAL
findings.evidence: src/modules/members/infrastructure/db/drizzle-member-repo.ts:935-944 — method calls `runInTenant(ctx, async (tx) => tx.execute(sql`...`))` which correctly sets `app.current_tenant`. However, `audit_log` RLS policy in migration 0007 uses `USING (tenant_id IS NULL OR tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant', TRUE))` — the NULL-OR branch means audit_log is NOT exclusively scoped by RLS alone. The query adds an explicit `WHERE tenant_id = ${ctx.slug}` (line 939) as defence-in-depth, which is correct. But the query does NOT include the member_id predicate as an index-matching expression that would let the planner select the (now dead) partial index efficiently once fixed. With the corrected index, the query plan would need: `tenant_id = ? AND event_type = 'member_plan_changed' AND payload ->> 'member_id' = ?` — all three predicates must match the index definition. The current query does include all three at lines 939-941, so after fixing the index key this becomes a legitimate index scan. No isolation regression — this is a combined perf + correctness note.
findings.recommendation: After applying the index fix (finding 1), verify the query plan confirms an Index Scan. No code change required here beyond documenting that `audit_log` RLS is permissive for NULL tenant_id rows and the explicit predicate in this query is load-bearing defence-in-depth (add a comment at drizzle-member-repo.ts:939 if not already present).

findings.title: searchDirectory correlated EXISTS subquery for q-filter not guarded by the contacts_tenant_member_idx partial index — potential secondary seq scan on non-removed contacts
findings.evidence: src/modules/members/infrastructure/db/drizzle-member-repo.ts:160-167 — directoryQFilter EXISTS uses `c.tenant_id = ${members.tenantId} AND c.member_id = ${members.memberId} AND c.removed_at IS NULL`. drizzle/migrations/0009_members_contacts.sql:87 creates `contacts_tenant_member_idx` ON contacts(tenant_id, member_id) WHERE removed_at IS NULL. This index should be usable for the EXISTS probe on (tenant_id, member_id) with the removed_at partial filter. However the additional ILIKE conditions on first_name, last_name, email cannot be satisfied by btree — they need trgm. The planner will use the btree index to narrow to the member's contacts, then evaluate the ILIKE conditions on the resulting heap rows. This is correct and bounded by contacts-per-member cardinality (~1-10). Not a blocking concern. The missing email trgm index (finding 2) is the dominant gap.
findings.recommendation: No action needed beyond the email trgm index in finding 2. Document that the EXISTS probe is bounded by contacts-per-member cardinality.

findings.title: list() cursor predicate direction is inverted relative to sort order — pages 2+ return wrong rows
findings.evidence: src/modules/invoicing/infrastructure/repos/drizzle-invoice-repo.ts:360 uses `gt(invoices.invoiceId, opts.cursor)` (invoice_id > cursor, ascending). The same query at line 367 orders by `desc(invoices.issueDate), desc(invoices.invoiceId)` (descending). Keyset pagination with a descending sort requires the cursor filter to be `lt` (invoice_id < cursor), not `gt`. A `gt` filter on a desc-sorted result set seeks FORWARD from the cursor — which is a different logical page than the continuation of the descending result — so page 2 returns a disjoint set of rows (likely duplicates of page 1 for small datasets, or a gap for larger ones).

findings.title: Five admin invoicing routes missing error.tsx boundaries
findings.evidence: No error.tsx exists under: src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/ · src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/[invoiceId]/ · src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/[invoiceId]/void/ · src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/[invoiceId]/credit-notes/new/ · src/app/(staff)/admin/settings/invoicing/. The admin detail page has three uncaught-throw hotspots: creditNoteRepo.findByOriginalInvoice (no .catch), Promise.all([resolveUserEmail x2]) inside userRepo.findById calls (no try/catch), and drizzleTenantSettingsRepo.getForIssue on the draft branch. All bubble to the root admin/error.tsx which uses DetailContainer — a container width mismatch that breaks CLS and the layout.
findings.recommendation: Add route-level error.tsx at each of the five paths. Each should use the matching layout container (TableContainer for list, DetailContainer for detail/void/credit-note, FormContainer for settings) and follow the PortalRouteError / AdminRouteError pattern. Include a back-to-invoices link and a reset() retry CTA.

findings.title: export-paid-invoices-csv: list failure cause silently dropped — Neon transients unobservable in production
findings.evidence: src/modules/invoicing/application/use-cases/export-paid-invoices-csv.ts:143-148 — the `catch` block for `listPaged` swallows the exception entirely and returns `err({ code: 'list_failed' })` with an inline comment 'we intentionally drop the cause object'. There is no `logger.error` or `logger.warn` call inside this catch, so when a Neon connection pool exhaustion or RLS misconfig causes the list to fail, the only observable signal is the route layer's 5xx response. The cause (SQLSTATE, connection error, etc.) is lost at the use-case boundary.

findings.title: Plaintext recipient email in audit_log.summary persists for 10 years
findings.evidence: src/modules/invoicing/application/use-cases/resend-pdf.ts:249 — `summary: \`Invoice ${documentNumber} PDF resent to ${recipientEmail}\``; line 265 — same for receipt variant; line 342 — same for credit note. The summary column is written to audit_log via f4AuditAdapter (infrastructure/adapters/audit-adapter.ts:56 `${event.summary}`). invoice_pdf_resent / receipt_pdf_resent / credit_note_pdf_resent carry retention_years=10 (audit-port.ts:122,123,124). Code comment at resend-pdf.ts:245-248 incorrectly calls the summary 'transient — not retained as a structured PII field'.
findings.recommendation: Replace plaintext email in all three summary strings with the already-computed sha256 prefix or a fixed placeholder: `\`Invoice ${documentNumber} PDF resent to [member email]\`` (the payload's recipient_email_sha256 carries the correlatable hash). Update the code comment at resend-pdf.ts:245 to remove the incorrect 'transient' claim. Add a unit assertion that audit.emit receives no '@' character in summary for invoice_pdf_resent / receipt_pdf_resent / credit_note_pdf_resent calls.

findings.title: serialiseInvoice returns internal Vercel Blob object keys (pdf_blob_key, receipt_pdf_blob_key) to admin API consumers
findings.evidence: src/app/api/invoices/_serialise.ts:48 — `pdf_blob_key: invoice.pdf?.blobKey ?? null`; line 56 — `receipt_pdf_blob_key: invoice.receiptPdf?.blobKey ?? null`. These fields are returned by GET /api/invoices/[invoiceId], GET /api/invoices, and GET /api/members/[memberId]/invoices (all admin-only). The blob key format is `invoicing/{tenantSlug}/invoices/{uuid}/{type}-v{n}.pdf`.
findings.recommendation: Consider omitting pdf_blob_key and receipt_pdf_blob_key from the admin serialiser response, or replacing them with a boolean `has_pdf` / `has_receipt_pdf` flag. The admin UI uses the dedicated download route (/api/invoices/[id]/pdf), not the raw blob key. If the key is needed for operator debugging, gate it behind a debug flag or return it only in the F9 audit export path.

findings.title: Cross-tenant invoice probe integration test exercises missing-ID path but not a real two-tenant scenario
findings.evidence: tests/integration/invoicing/void-invoice.test.ts:506-532 — the cross-tenant probe test calls voidInvoice with `invoiceId: randomUUID()` (guaranteed not found in any tenant), not with a real invoiceId belonging to a second test tenant. The Principle I sub-clause 3 requirement is: 'cross-tenant probe test: is there an integration test that tries to read/write another tenant's row and asserts failure.'
findings.recommendation: Add an integration test that seeds an invoice under tenant-A, then calls findById (or getInvoice / voidInvoice) using tenant-B's runInTenant context with tenant-A's real invoiceId, and asserts the result is null (not found). Pattern already exists in F3 members (tests/integration/members/us3-tenant-isolation.test.ts). The test can reuse createTestTenant() + createTestMember() helpers.

findings.title: Success-state icons hardcode emerald instead of the canonical --success token
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/[invoiceId]/_components/payment-timeline.tsx:131, :137, :142 use `text-emerald-700 dark:text-emerald-400` for the payment_succeeded / invoice_paid / refund_succeeded timeline icons; src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/[invoiceId]/_components/copy-charge-id-button.tsx:66 uses the same emerald pair for the copy-success check icon. These are F5 Phase 5 files (dated 2026-04-26) authored before the semantic color tokens were added. `src/app/globals.css:165-167` (light) and the dark block (`--success: oklch(0.68 0.16 145)`) now define `--success`/`--success-foreground`/`--success-surface`, and `@theme` maps `--color-success`, so `text-success` is a live Tailwind v4 utility. The F7 broadcast module already consumes the sibling token correctly (e.g. `src/components/broadcast/quota-display.tsx:186` `text-warning`), so invoicing is the lagging adopter, not a token-availability gap.
findings.recommendation: Replace `text-emerald-700 dark:text-emerald-400` with `text-success` in both files (4 sites). This collapses the manual light/dark pair into one token and aligns invoicing with broadcasts. Contrast is preserved — `--success` is oklch(0.52 0.15 145) light / oklch(0.68 0.16 145) dark, comparable to emerald-700/emerald-400, and the original tuning comment's ≥4.5:1 goal is met by the token. Add an eslint `no-restricted-syntax` or a `check:` grep guard banning raw `emerald|green-[0-9]` in invoice components to prevent regression.

findings.title: Logo upload confirms via opaque blob-key text, not a visual preview
findings.evidence: src/components/invoices/invoice-settings-form.tsx:530-538 — after a tenant logo is uploaded, the only confirmation is `logo.currentKey: ` rendered as text (`{logoBlobKey}`). There is no `` thumbnail of the uploaded logo. This is the brand asset that prints on every legal Thai tax invoice/receipt PDF.
findings.recommendation: Render a small bordered thumbnail (`` from the signed blob URL, e.g. max-h-16, on a neutral `bg-muted` chip so a transparent-PNG logo stays visible in dark mode) next to or instead of the raw key string. Keep the key for power-user/debug but make the primary confirmation visual.

findings.title: Admin list and detail formatDate bypasses next-intl, risks inconsistent TH calendar output
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/[invoiceId]/page.tsx:106-112 (local `formatDate` function) and src/app/(member)/portal/invoices/_utils/format.ts:32-37 both use `new Date(iso).toLocaleDateString(locale, { year: 'numeric', month: 'short', day: 'numeric' })` without specifying `calendar: 'gregory'`. Under some ICU builds (Node 22 LTS ships full ICU by default), `'th'` locale defaults to the Buddhist Era calendar, producing year 2569 instead of 2026. This contradicts the project convention that BE is display-only and must be explicitly opted-in, and produces a different year label than next-intl's `formatDateTime` (which the project uses everywhere else and which defaults to Gregorian).
findings.recommendation: Replace the bespoke `formatDate` helpers with next-intl's `formatDateTime` (server components) or `useFormatter().dateTime` (client components), or explicitly pass `calendar: 'gregory'` to the Intl.DateTimeFormat options. The canonical pattern `formatDateTime(new Date(iso), { dateStyle: 'medium' })` already handles locale + calendar correctly project-wide.

findings.title: CSV export dialog validation error not associated to date inputs (WCAG SC 1.3.1 / 3.3.1)
findings.evidence:
src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/_components/csv-export-dialog.tsx:112-130 — the #csv-export-from and #csv-export-to inputs have no aria-describedby and no aria-invalid. When validation fails (invalid date, inverted range, range >1 year), a `

` renders with the error text. However, neither input is programmatically linked to the error paragraph, so screen readers navigating by form field do not surface the validation message when focus is on the date inputs.
findings.recommendation: Add `aria-describedby='csv-export-error'` and `aria-invalid={error !== null}` to both date inputs when an error is present. Use conditional concatenation: `aria-describedby={error ? 'csv-export-error' : undefined}` to avoid pointing to a non-existent element when there is no error.

findings.title: Credit note form reason field missing aria-invalid (submit-blocked state invisible to SR)
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/[invoiceId]/credit-notes/new/_components/credit-note-form.tsx:171-182 — the reason `<Textarea id='cn-reason'>` has `aria-describedby='cn-reason-help'` (pointing to the character count) but no `aria-invalid`. The Submit button is disabled via `canSubmit = amountValid && reasonValid && matches && !pending`. When the reason field is empty and the user tries to submit, the button stays disabled with no audible cue on the reason field. The amount field (line 153) correctly has `aria-invalid={exceedsRemainder || (amountThb.length > 0 && !amountValid)}` but the reason field does not.
findings.recommendation:
Add `aria-invalid={reason.trim().length === 0 && reason.length > 0}` to the reason Textarea (match the touched-and-empty pattern) and add a visible inline error `

` that appears when the form is submitted with an empty reason. Alternatively align with the refund form's `react-hook-form + touchedFields` pattern for consistency.

findings.title: ilike search on credit_notes.document_number also unindexed
findings.evidence: drizzle-credit-note-repo.ts:341: `ilike(creditNotes.documentNumber, '%${input.search.trim()}%')` with leading wildcard. Migration 0019 defines only `credit_notes_tenant_fiscal_seq_unique` and `credit_notes_tenant_original_idx` on credit_notes — no document_number index. Credit-note volume is lower than invoices (~1 CN per partially-credited invoice) so blast radius is smaller than P1 above, but the pattern is identical.
findings.recommendation: Same fix as P1 finding: `CREATE INDEX credit_notes_document_number_trgm_idx ON credit_notes USING gin (document_number gin_trgm_ops);` Layer: Infrastructure (migration). Can be batched in the same migration as the invoices fix.

findings.title: listPaged COUNT(*) uses raw SQL string with repeated filter construction — potential plan-cache drift
findings.evidence: drizzle-invoice-repo.ts:448-450: `tx.execute(sql\`SELECT COUNT(*)::int AS c FROM invoices WHERE ${and(...filters)!}\`)` runs as raw SQL parallel to the main SELECT. The `and(...filters)` is constructed twice — once for the Drizzle ORM SELECT (line 441) and once for the raw COUNT (line 449). The raw SQL bypasses Drizzle's prepared-statement path, meaning Neon will parse+plan this query fresh on every call rather than caching the plan as it does for parameterised ORM queries. At 100 req/s, this is 100 extra plan-cache misses/second.
findings.recommendation: Replace the raw `tx.execute(sql\`SELECT COUNT(*)\`)` with a Drizzle `tx.select({ c: sql\`COUNT(*)::int\` }).from(invoices).where(and(...filters))` call. This uses the same parameterised path as the main SELECT, eliminates plan-cache misses, and makes filter divergence a compile-time type error rather than a runtime surprise. Layer: Infrastructure (drizzle-invoice-repo.ts). The credit-note repo's `listPaged` COUNT at drizzle-credit-note-repo.ts:377-380 already uses the Drizzle ORM path correctly — use that as the template.
