findings.title: CSV export (export-paid-invoices-csv) pages through ALL paid invoices for the tenant, O(N) RTTs
findings.evidence: export-paid-invoices-csv.ts:87: PAGE_SIZE=100. Lines 128-148: `do { listPaged(offset, 100); offset += 100; } while (offset < total)`. For a tenant with 1,300 paid invoices (131 members × 10 years), this is 13 sequential RTTs to Neon, each involving the parallel main-SELECT + COUNT(*) query pair (drizzle-invoice-repo.ts:440-484). Each RTT to Neon Singapore from Vercel sin1 is ~2-5ms on a warm connection; at 13 RTTs that is 26-65ms of pure network overhead. At 5k paid invoices the loop makes 50 RTTs. The date-range filter is applied IN MEMORY after fetch (lines 136-139) so a narrow 1-month export still scans the full paid partition. The use-case comment at lines 13-18 acknowledges this and cites 1.3k rows/year as acceptable — that estimate is per-year, not per-tenant-lifetime. After 5 years of operation this becomes 6.5k paid rows and 65 RTTs.
findings.recommendation: Add `paid_at` range columns to the `listPaged` filter (or create a dedicated `listPaidInRange` repo method) so the date filter is pushed into the DB WHERE clause. A single query with `WHERE status='paid' AND paid_at BETWEEN $from AND $to` replaces N paginated calls with one (or a small number of) pages. The index `invoices_tenant_status_issued_idx` covers `(tenant_id, status)` but not `paid_at` — add a partial index `CREATE INDEX invoices_tenant_paid_at_idx ON invoices (tenant_id, paid_at) WHERE status = 'paid'` in the same migration batch. Layer: Infrastructure (new repo method + migration); Application (update use-case to pass range params).

findings.title: GET /api/plans fires an extra getTenantTaxPolicy RTT (reads tenant_invoice_settings) on every list-plans call
findings.evidence: plans-deps.ts:57-67: `taxPolicy: async () => { const policy = await getTenantTaxPolicy(makeGetTenantTaxPolicyDeps(), tenant.slug); ... }`. The `getTenantTaxPolicy` facade calls `tenantSettingsRepo.getForIssue(tenantId)` which is `runInTenant(ctx, tx => tx.select().from(tenantInvoiceSettings).where(...).limit(1))` — a full `SET LOCAL` + SELECT round-trip to Neon. This fires on every `GET /api/plans` request regardless of filter, before the plan list query itself. With Neon Singapore latency ~2-5ms per RTT, this adds 2-10ms on the plans list hot path. The SLO for plans list is p95 < 400ms (observability.md §11). The extra RTT is serial (taxPolicy() resolves before planRepo.findByTenantAndYear()). Previously recorded in agent memory (project_f2_plans_perf_findings.md).
findings.recommendation: Cache the tenant tax policy for the request lifetime (it is read-only, immutable within a request). The simplest fix: call `taxPolicy()` and `planRepo.findByTenantAndYear()` in parallel with `Promise.all` inside `listPlans`. The taxPolicy result is needed only for the `map()` after the plan query resolves, so concurrent fetch saves the serial RTT. Layer: Application (list-plans.ts). Alternatively add a short-lived (e.g. 60s) in-memory cache keyed by tenant_slug in the composition root, acceptable because tax-policy changes are rare admin actions that trigger an explicit settings update.

findings.title: applyReceiptPdf and applyReceiptPdfFailure each load invoice_lines in a second query after UPDATE — extra RTT on async receipt worker hot path
findings.evidence: drizzle-invoice-repo.ts:678-688 (applyReceiptPdf): after the UPDATE, fetches the invoice row (line 664-671) then fetches invoice_lines (line 672-688). drizzle-invoice-repo.ts:743-783 (applyReceiptPdfFailure happy-path): same pattern — UPDATE + re-fetch row + re-fetch lines. The receipt-PDF worker (renderReceiptPdf use-case) calls applyReceiptPdf on success or applyReceiptPdfFailure on error; both paths incur 2 extra SELECT RTTs. The async receipt-render cron dispatches one of these per paid invoice. SLO-F5-007 requires receipt email first-attempt ≤ 60s post-webhook-ack, with the outbox cron at 1-min prod cadence; the worker latency directly erodes this budget.
findings.recommendation: The UPDATE...RETURNING for applyReceiptPdf already returns the updated invoice row. The invoice_lines for that specific invoice are immutable once issued (immutability trigger blocks changes to line content after issue). Cache the lines from the initial applyPayment call or fetch them once in the worker before calling the repo method and pass them in, avoiding the re-fetch. Alternatively, if the caller (render-receipt-pdf use-case) already has the Invoice object (it does — it passes `invoice.invoiceId`), pass the existing `invoice.lines` to the repo method and skip the lines re-fetch. Layer: Infrastructure (drizzle-invoice-repo.ts) or Application (render-receipt-pdf.ts).

findings.title: `idempotencyKey` field on `recordPayment` accepted but silently ignored — F5 webhook retries rely on status-only replay detection
findings.evidence: src/modules/invoicing/application/use-cases/record-payment.ts:64-76 — `idempotencyKey: z.string().min(1).max(200).optional()` is in the Zod schema but a comment at line 67 explicitly states 'CURRENTLY IGNORED by this use-case'. The field is reserved for a 'Phase-10 enhancement'. The current deduplication relies solely on `lockedStatus === 'paid'` short-circuit (line 241).
findings.recommendation: The existing spec tasks.md tracks this as Phase-10 polish. No action needed at launch — document the limitation in the runbook and monitor for any duplicate `invoice_paid` audit events post-launch. Re-prioritise the idempotency-key persistence as P1 before F5 webhook volume grows beyond ~100 concurrent events/minute.

findings.title: US7 AS2: F3 member-timeline integration is wired in the barrel but F4 event types carry `member_id` only for some paths — `invoice_voided` emit site marks `member_id` but void itself shows the right payload in the audit
findings.evidence: src/modules/invoicing/index.ts:99-106 defines `F4_MEMBER_TIMELINE_EVENT_TYPES` including `invoice_voided` and `invoice_pdf_resent`. The barrel comment at line 88 explicitly marks these as 'Deferred emit sites'. `void-invoice.ts:256` emits `invoice_voided` with `member_id: loaded.memberId` in payload — this is correctly wired. `resend-pdf.ts:239-258` emits `invoice_pdf_resent` with `member_id: invoice.memberId` — also correctly wired. The barrel comment is outdated (the emit sites exist). However, `receipt_pdf_resent` and `credit_note_pdf_resent` deliberately exclude `member_id` per design decision at index.ts:100-105.
findings.recommendation: Update the barrel comment at src/modules/invoicing/index.ts:88-95 to remove the 'Deferred emit sites' notice for `invoice_voided` and `invoice_pdf_resent` — both now have real emitters. This is a documentation fix only.

findings.title: FR-026 email delivery failure surfaced to admin — `auto_email_delivery_failed` audit event exists in port but no evidence of UI surface on invoice detail page
findings.evidence: src/modules/invoicing/application/ports/audit-port.ts:36 declares `auto_email_delivery_failed`. FR-026 requires the system to 'surface the failure to admins on the invoice detail page with a clear action to retry or edit the recipient'. Reading src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/[invoiceId]/page.tsx (not read in full) is needed to confirm, but the audit event type's 5y retention (not 10y) and absence of a dedicated UI component in the glob output suggests no failure-notification banner on the invoice detail screen.
findings.recommendation: Read the invoice detail page (`src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/[invoiceId]/page.tsx`) to confirm whether a delivery-failure badge is rendered. If absent, add an `auto_email_delivery_failed` query on the invoice's outbox rows and surface an inline alert with a 'Resend' CTA on the detail page. This pairs with the existing `resend-pdf` use-case.

findings.title: Excess: `export-paid-invoices-csv` in-memory full-tenant page-scan is a latency risk at any tenant with >1k paid invoices
findings.evidence: src/modules/invoicing/application/use-cases/export-paid-invoices-csv.ts:87-148 — fetches ALL paid invoices for a tenant in 100-row pages (`status: 'paid'`) then post-filters by `paidAt` date range in memory. Comment at line 16 acknowledges this: 'For chamber scale (~131 members × ~10 invoices/year ≈ 1.3k rows/year), fetching all paid rows for the tenant and post-filtering in memory costs <1MB / <50ms'. This is correct for SweCham but the same code is deployed as multi-tenant SaaS.
findings.recommendation: Acceptable at launch per the documented trade-off. Add a `paidAt >= from AND paidAt <= to` filter directly to `InvoiceRepo.listPaged` before onboarding a second tenant with >2k invoices. Track as a database scalability improvement for F10/multi-tenant readiness.

findings.title: RefundForm reason field: inline error not referenced by aria-describedby — AT cannot announce validation error
findings.evidence:
src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/[invoiceId]/_components/refund-dialog/refund-form.tsx:306 — Textarea has aria-describedby={reasonHelpId} only; when reasonError is truthy, an inline

with role='alert' is rendered (line 324 pattern mirrors amount field at L282) but the Textarea's aria-describedby is never extended to reference the error paragraph's id. Compare: amount field at L267 similarly only points to help, not to the error

.
findings.recommendation: Generate a stable errorId (e.g. `${reasonId}-error`), render the error paragraph with that id, and set aria-describedby={reasonError ? `${reasonHelpId} ${reasonId}-error` : reasonHelpId}. Apply the same fix to the amount field (amountId-error). This matches the TypedPhraseConfirm pattern at refund-form.tsx:57.

findings.title: PaymentTimeline formatTimestamp uses bare toLocaleString without explicit timezone — Bangkok events drift under UTC server offset
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/[invoiceId]/_components/payment-timeline.tsx:101-110 — formatTimestamp calls date.toLocaleString(locale, { ..., timeZoneName: 'short' }) with no timeZone option. All timestamps are stored as ISO 8601 UTC (CLAUDE.md § Conventions). When the Next.js server process runs in UTC (Vercel sin1), toLocaleString without timezone renders UTC time. For a Bangkok user at 15:00 ICT, the event will display as '08:00 UTC' with abbreviation 'GMT+7' only if the browser runs this — but this is a Server Component, so the server locale determines output. The in-file comment at L96 claims 'Modern V8 returns BE for th-TH by default' but does NOT address the timezone gap (UTC server vs Bangkok display).
findings.recommendation: Pass { timeZone: 'Asia/Bangkok' } to the toLocaleString options object in formatTimestamp. For sv locale keep UTC or add a tenant-configurable timezone. Alternatively use next-intl formatDateTime(date, { dateStyle: 'medium', timeStyle: 'short', timeZone: 'Asia/Bangkok' }) consistently with F4's formatDate helper.

findings.title: processChargeRefunded: getRefundContextForUpdate called before lockForUpdate — stale snapshot window
findings.evidence: src/modules/payments/application/use-cases/process-charge-refunded.ts:286-295 — in the webhook-recovery path for a `pending` in-app refund row, the code calls `deps.refundsRepo.getRefundContextForUpdate(tx, ...)` (line 286) before calling `deps.paymentsRepo.lockForUpdate(tx, ...)` (line 291). The aggregate (succeededSumSatang) is read before the parent payment row is locked. A concurrent `issueRefund` Phase B that commits between these two lines could advance `succeededSumSatang` in the DB; the subsequently acquired FOR UPDATE snapshot sees the updated payment row but the `ctx` used to derive `isFullyRefunded` is stale.

findings.title: drizzle-refunds-repo.updateStatus always throws on zero-row match — no null return for expectedCurrentStatus race guard
findings.evidence: src/modules/payments/infrastructure/repos/drizzle-refunds-repo.ts:150-163 — `updateStatus` throws `new Error('drizzle-refunds-repo: updateStatus matched zero rows...')` unconditionally when 0 rows are matched, regardless of whether `expectedCurrentStatus` was provided as a race guard. Contrast with `drizzle-payments-repo.updateStatus` (lines 310-318) which returns `null` when `expectedCurrentStatus` is set and 0 rows match, or throws only when no guard was supplied.

findings.title: e.message logged in 6 pino catch sites across payments/cancel/refund initiate routes
findings.evidence: src/app/api/payments/initiate/route.ts:131 — `requireMemberContext` unexpected-throw catch: `err: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)`. Same pattern at :194 (rate-limit audit-write failure) and :332 (use-case unexpected throw). src/app/api/payments/[id]/cancel/route.ts:82 (member-context throw), :136 (rate-limit audit-write failure), :216 (use-case unexpected throw). src/app/api/refunds/initiate/route.ts:255 (use-case unexpected throw).
findings.recommendation: Apply the same H-4 pattern used consistently in the webhook route: replace `e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)` with `e instanceof Error ? e.constructor.name : 'unknown'` at all 7 catch sites. The webhook route (`src/app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts`) is the reference. OTel distributed traces already provide full call-chain context; pino does not need the raw message.

findings.title: POST /api/refunds/initiate rate-limit hit emits no audit event
findings.evidence: src/app/api/refunds/initiate/route.ts:141-153 — on `!rl.success` branch the route returns 429 with no call to `f5AuditAdapter.emit`. Contrast: `src/app/api/payments/initiate/route.ts:181-200` emits `payment_initiate_rate_limited` and `src/app/api/payments/[id]/cancel/route.ts:123-143` emits `payment_cancel_rate_limited`, both explicitly. The refund route is the only F5 rate-limited surface without a forensic audit trail on rate-limit hits.
findings.recommendation: Add a `f5AuditAdapter.emit(null, { eventType: 'refund_initiate_rate_limited', ... })` call inside the `!rl.success` branch of `/api/refunds/initiate/route.ts` (after the logger.warn at line 148, before the errorResponse at line 152). Mirror the exact payload shape used by the payment-initiate route. Wrap in try/catch and log failure without re-throwing so the 429 is never blocked by an audit write failure.

findings.title: Payment timeline success-event icons bypass the --success token (raw emerald-700/400)
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/[invoiceId]/_components/payment-timeline.tsx:131,137,143 — EVENT_VISUAL maps payment_succeeded / invoice_paid / refund_succeeded to cls: 'text-emerald-700 dark:text-emerald-400'. Same raw emerald appears in copy-charge-id-button.tsx:66. The design system ships dedicated success tokens (src/app/globals.css:165-167 light, 288-290 dark: --success / --success-foreground / --success-surface, mapped to text-success/bg-success at globals.css:82-84) and the canonical primitives use them (status-badge.tsx:30 'text-success', inline-alert.tsx:35, status-dot.tsx:20, progress.tsx:30). The audit standard (docs/go-live-readiness.md:31) explicitly calls for 'no hardcoded colors / arbitrary values bypassing tokens'.
findings.recommendation: Replace 'text-emerald-700 dark:text-emerald-400' with 'text-success' in payment-timeline.tsx (3 EVENT_VISUAL entries) and copy-charge-id-button.tsx. Removes the hand-rolled dual-value contrast comment since the token already guarantees both-theme contrast. NOTE: this is a codebase-wide pattern (events/, renewals/, dashboard/ all use raw emerald) — fixing only payments is fine for go-live; a system-wide emerald→success sweep is the larger backlog item.

findings.title: Test-mode badge uses arbitrary text-[10px] instead of the type scale
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/[invoiceId]/_components/payment-timeline.tsx:408 — <Badge variant="secondary" className="text-[10px] uppercase"> for the TEST-mode chip. The project mandates the .text-h1../.text-caption scale; text-caption is the established smallest token. The same arbitrary text-[10px] recurs at invoice-table.tsx:386 (credit-note chip).
findings.recommendation: Use text-caption (or text-xs) + the existing Badge sizing rather than text-[10px]. If a visually-smaller chip is intentional, encode it as a Badge size variant in the primitive instead of an arbitrary per-call value, so the scale stays centralized.

findings.title: i18n key portal.payment.retry.alternativeMethod defined in all three locales but never rendered in PaymentFailurePanel
findings.evidence: src/i18n/messages/en.json:4227 and th.json:4227 and sv.json:4227 each define 'alternativeMethod': 'Use another method' / 'ใช้วิธีชำระเงินอื่น' / 'Använd en annan metod'. Grep of src/app for alternativeMethod returns zero rendering call sites. PaymentFailurePanel (card-form.tsx:164-187) shows only 'Try again' (retry.cta) with no secondary 'Use another method' affordance. The MethodTabs remain visible during failure state (pay-sheet-internal.tsx:800-801 confirms this) so tab-switching is the implicit affordance, but no explicit CTA guides the user there.
findings.recommendation: Add an 'alternativeMethod' secondary <Button variant="outline"> to PaymentFailurePanel (or inside PaySheetInternal's failure branch) that calls onMethodChange to toggle to the other method. Only render when enabledMethods.length > 1. Wire t('retry.alternativeMethod') as label. Scope: post-launch-backlog if launch is card-only; auto-fix if PromptPay launches simultaneously.

findings.title: ConfirmationPanel 5-second auto-close: WCAG 2.2.1 pause button is 24px — below SC 2.5.8 minimum 24×24px with insufficient surrounding space
findings.evidence:
src/app/(member)/portal/invoices/[invoiceId]/_components/pay-sheet/confirmation-panel.tsx:229-244 — Pause/Resume button has className min-h-[24px] min-w-[24px], which satisfies SC 2.5.8's 24×24 minimum but only when nothing intrudes into the 24px spacing offset zone. The button sits in a flex row immediately adjacent to the countdown

text. SC 2.5.8 requires that when the target is exactly 24×24, an 'offset' of at least (24-actual)/2 px must be clear of other targets. Inline with an adjacent text node, that offset is almost certainly violated on narrow viewports.
findings.recommendation: Raise to min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px] to match primary CTAs and remove ambiguity. Alternatively move the pause button to its own line below the countdown text to guarantee the offset clearance. This is the same fix applied elsewhere in the codebase (status-panel.tsx L79, pay-sheet/index.tsx L447).

findings.title: TypedPhraseConfirm hardcodes English 'REFUND' prefix — not localised for TH/SV admin users
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/[invoiceId]/_components/refund-dialog/refund-form.tsx:149 — const expectedPhrase = `REFUND ${memberCompanyName}`. The 'REFUND' string is hardcoded in English. Thai admins using th locale must type the English word 'REFUND' regardless of their UI language.
findings.recommendation: Extract a translation key e.g. admin.refund.form.typedPhrase.prefix = 'REFUND' (EN), 'REFUND' (TH intentionally same per Thai finance convention), 'REFUND' (SV intentionally same). Derive expectedPhrase as `${t('prefix')} ${memberCompanyName}` so the canonical value has one source and can be updated without touching product code. Alternatively document explicitly in the i18n file that the prefix is intentionally language-neutral (like 'VOID' in the void dialog).

findings.title: SecurityFooter privacy disclosure <a> (Stripe privacy link) has no accessible name beyond its text content — focus ring uses underline only, not ring-2
findings.evidence: src/app/(member)/portal/invoices/[invoiceId]/_components/pay-sheet/security-footer.tsx:39-51 — the Stripe privacy link uses className='underline hover:text-foreground' with no focus-visible:ring-2 / focus-visible:outline classes. Compare payment-timeline.tsx:422 which correctly uses focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2 rounded-sm on the 'View in Stripe' link.
findings.recommendation: Add focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-1 focus-visible:rounded-sm to the anchor className in security-footer.tsx.

findings.title: PaymentTimeline empty state 'Record payment manually' link uses fragment href (#record-payment) — unreliable scroll on Safari + broken if element not in DOM
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/[invoiceId]/_components/payment-timeline.tsx:469-476 — <a href={`/admin/invoices/${invoiceId}#record-payment`}> targets the RecordPaymentDialog trigger element which has id='record-payment'. On the same page this is an in-page anchor. Scroll-anchoring with sticky headers (DetailContainer layout) may obscure the target. Additionally, if the staff role check hides the RecordPaymentDialog trigger (isAdmin=false branch) the link navigates to an id that does not exist.
findings.recommendation: Wrap the fragment link with scroll-margin-top or use router.push + sheet trigger programmatic open instead of href fragment. Also gate the link render more tightly: it is already conditionally rendered only when isAdmin && invoiceStatus === 'issued', but verify the RecordPaymentDialog trigger is always in DOM when isAdmin is true.

findings.title: PaySheetInternal aria-live announcer is role=status — 'success' state announcement may be politely queued behind ongoing SR speech
findings.evidence: src/app/(member)/portal/invoices/[invoiceId]/_components/pay-sheet/pay-sheet-internal.tsx:809-816 — the persistent live region uses aria-live='polite' role='status'. derivePayStateAnnouncement returns t('success.title') when kind==='success'. However success is a high-importance moment; if the SR is mid-sentence on other content (e.g. reading the ConfirmationPanel that just mounted), the polite queue may delay or drop the announcement on NVDA.
findings.recommendation: No immediate code change required. For the success transition only, consider switching the announcer text to an aria-live='assertive' sibling (or upgrading role to 'alert') at the moment kind transitions to 'success'. However, this risks double-announce if ConfirmationPanel's focus also reads the heading. Acceptable to leave as-is at launch with a follow-up task to run NVDA regression.

findings.title: member_invite_to_payment_funnel_dropoff counter defined but never called
findings.evidence: src/lib/metrics.ts:998-1012 defines `paymentsMetrics.inviteToPaymentFunnelStep(tenantId, step)` which emits `payments_member_invite_to_payment_funnel_dropoff`. Grep across all of src/ (excluding metrics.ts itself) returns zero call sites. docs/observability.md:786 lists this metric as part of the 18-metric F5 catalogue. It is documented as the 'F5.1 promotion KPI (FR-016a)' gating the `allow_anonymous_paylink` feature.
findings.recommendation: This is an F5.1 (post-MVP) surface per the spec. Wire `inviteToPaymentFunnelStep` at the six funnel checkpoints as they are built: `invite_sent` at `POST /api/auth/invite` (when invitation context indicates payment funnel); `payment_initiated` and `payment_succeeded` are already instrumentable in `initiatePayment` (src/modules/payments/application/use-cases/initiate-payment.ts:601) and `confirmPayment` respectively — add the step call alongside the existing `paymentsMetrics.initiateCount` / `paymentsMetrics.succeededCount` calls. The remaining steps (`invite_opened`, `account_created`, `invoice_viewed`) require portal-event instrumentation and are F5.1 scope. Do not block go-live on the full funnel — wire `payment_initiated` and `payment_succeeded` steps now as a minimum.

findings.title: FR-021 funnel-dropoff metric inviteToPaymentFunnelStep defined but never emitted
findings.evidence: src/lib/metrics.ts:998-1012 — inviteToPaymentFunnelStep() instrument defined with steps invite_sent, invite_opened, account_created, invoice_viewed, payment_initiated, payment_succeeded. No call site found anywhere in src/ beyond the definition — confirmed by grep returning only metrics.ts:998.
findings.recommendation: Wire emit calls at the 4 currently-instrumentable checkpoints where F5-data already exists: (1) payment_initiated — inside initiatePayment use-case after the paymentsMetrics.initiateCount call (src/modules/payments/application/use-cases/initiate-payment.ts:601); (2) payment_succeeded — inside confirmPayment; (3) invoice_viewed — inside the portal invoice page server component or loadInvoicePaymentActivity; (4) invite_sent — inside invite-user-for-member.ts. The invite_opened step requires email-open tracking (post-MVP). Schedule the wiring as a separate post-launch task; it does not block go-live but must be done before the 30-day SC-001a review.

findings.title: isRefundTransition reads pre-lock snapshot of existingReg.ticket.paymentStatus
findings.evidence: src/modules/events/application/use-cases/_helpers/process-attendee-in-tx.ts:823-827 — `const isRefundTransition = !regInsert.value.isNewRegistration && input.attendee.paymentStatus === 'refunded' && existingReg.ticket.paymentStatus !== 'refunded'`. The `existingReg` snapshot comes from the `insertOnConflictDoNothing` RETURNING clause (before the advisory lock at line 838 is acquired). A concurrent webhook/CSV that commits a `markRefunded` flip between the INSERT CONFLICT path and the lock acquisition will make the snapshot stale, causing the refund branch to short-circuit when it should fire (or vice versa).
findings.recommendation: Acceptable for launch at SweCham single-admin scale as documented. For F6.2: either (a) acquire a per-registration advisory lock unconditionally at the start of the refund-flip branch (before reading existingReg.ticket.paymentStatus), or (b) re-read the row inside the lock scope rather than relying on the INSERT RETURNING snapshot. Track as F6.2 backlog item.

findings.title: throw new Error in Application layer — invariant-violation guards in 4 use-cases
findings.evidence: archive-event.ts:374 (`throw new Error('archive-event invariant: memberId ... missing from pre-loop allotmentsCache')`), relink-registration.ts:574 and 616 (naturalAllotment < 0 invariant throws), toggle-event-category.ts:376 (same allotmentsCache miss pattern). All in src/modules/events/application/use-cases/.
findings.recommendation: For each site, either (a) convert to a custom InvariantViolationError class that extends Error and is documented as 'Application-internal fatal throw' in a JSDoc, or (b) replace with `return err({ kind: 'internal_invariant_violation', message: ... })` and handle at route layer as 500. Option (a) is minimal-change and consistent with the existing F5 pattern. File as F6.2 cleanup item; not a launch blocker.

findings.title: Double /** comment artifact corrupts JSDoc for safeEmitCancellationNoPrior
findings.evidence: src/modules/events/application/use-cases/import-csv.ts:1631-1632 — two consecutive `/**` tokens open the safeEmitCancellationNoPrior function's JSDoc. Line 1631: `/**` (no content, no closing `*/`). Line 1632: `/**` (the real block, closed at line 1636 `*/`). TypeScript compiles successfully because it treats the stray outer `/**` as a comment introducer immediately followed by the inner block, but doc-gen tools (TypeDoc) and some lint rules treating `/**` as a JSDoc marker will emit a warning or generate malformed docs.
findings.recommendation: Remove the stray `/**` on line 1631. One-character fix: `/**` → (delete the line). No logic change.

findings.title: wasAlreadyPseudonymised idempotency condition is non-obvious and partially redundant
findings.evidence: src/modules/events/application/use-cases/pseudonymise-stale-non-member-pii.ts:146-147 — `const wasAlreadyPseudonymised = updateResult.value.piiPseudonymisedAt !== null && updateResult.value.piiPseudonymisedAt.getTime() !== input.occurredAt.getTime()`. The condition relies on timestamp inequality to distinguish 'just written by us (equal)' from 'written by a prior sweep (different)'. It is correct but fragile: if a future caller passes the same `occurredAt` on retry AND a concurrent sweep already ran, the guard returns false (timestamps equal) and the audit double-emits.
findings.recommendation: Simplify the condition to `updateResult.value.piiPseudonymisedAt !== null` — if the repo returned a row with non-null piiPseudonymisedAt, it was already pseudonymised (the UPDATE wrote it or a concurrent writer did). The timestamp-inequality check is misleading and unnecessary. Add a code comment explaining the concurrent-sweep scenario.

findings.title: drizzleAttendeeMatcher singleton bound to root db exported without RLS guard
findings.evidence: src/modules/events/infrastructure/drizzle-attendee-matcher.ts:50-52, line 227 — the factory signature is `makeDrizzleAttendeeMatcher(executor: TenantTx | typeof db = db)` with a default of the root `db` pool (BYPASSRLS role), and a pre-built singleton `export const drizzleAttendeeMatcher = makeDrizzleAttendeeMatcher()` is exported. The singleton is bound to root db and will bypass RLS on any SELECT it issues against `contacts` and `members`. No call site currently consumes the singleton (grep confirmed only one match, in the same file), but the export is available to any future feature developer who imports from the infrastructure layer.
findings.recommendation: Remove the `export const drizzleAttendeeMatcher` singleton from drizzle-attendee-matcher.ts (line 227). Remove the default `= db` parameter value from the factory signature so callers are forced to supply an executor. The only valid callers are di.ts factories which already pass `tx`. Add an ESLint rule or a comment block making it explicit that this adapter must only be instantiated inside `runInTenant`. Optionally: enforce via the barrel (src/modules/events/index.ts) by not re-exporting this adapter at all — route handlers should always go through di.ts.

findings.title: attendeeEmailLower absent from pino REDACT_PATHS
findings.evidence: src/lib/logger.ts:45-340 — 'attendeeEmailLower' is not in REDACT_PATHS. src/modules/events/infrastructure/drizzle-registrations-repository.ts:75-78 — readAttendeeEmailLower() derives the lowercase email from the row's attendeeEmail. The field is present in the EventRegistrationRow type and logged via the aggregate in toAggregate(). The pino-audit-port.ts REDACT_ALLOWED_KEYS (lines 234-296) correctly omits it, but the upstream logger.ts has no entry for it.
findings.recommendation: Include 'attendeeEmailLower' and '*.attendeeEmailLower' in the REDACT_PATHS addition described for the attendeeEmail finding (P1 above). Can be done in a single commit.

findings.title: Webhook rate-limit step-4 metric label fires 'rejected_bad_sig' for pre-auth rate-limits
findings.evidence: src/app/api/webhooks/eventcreate/v1/[tenantSlug]/route.ts:364-385 — the rate-limit exceeded branch (Step 4) calls `eventcreateMetrics.webhookReceiptsTotal(tenantSlug, 'rejected_bad_sig', 'rate_limited')`. The label `rejected_bad_sig` is semantically incorrect for a rate-limit event (no signature was attempted) and is meant to represent signature-mismatch outcomes. The file-level comment at line 167-173 maps verify-kinds to `'rejected_bad_sig'` but rate_limiting is not a verify-kind — it fires before signature verification. This misclassifies rate-limited requests as 'rejected_bad_sig' in the OTel histogram.
findings.recommendation: Change line 364 from `'rejected_bad_sig'` to `'rejected_pre_auth'` to match the correct metric label used by the Content-Type and body-size pre-auth checks (lines 284-290, 314). The metric type definition already includes `'rejected_pre_auth'` as a valid label (see its usage at line 284).

findings.title: CSV-import degraded warning banners hand-roll amber instead of the InlineAlert warning primitive
findings.evidence: src/components/events/csv-import-result.tsx:193, :212, :231 — three identical inline banners 'border border-amber-300 bg-amber-50 p-2 dark:border-amber-700 dark:bg-amber-950/40' with 'text-amber-700 dark:text-amber-400' icon and 'text-amber-900 dark:text-amber-200' copy, plus :332 Counter success tone 'text-emerald-700 dark:text-emerald-300'. The canonical InlineAlert (src/components/ui/inline-alert.tsx) exists precisely for this (tone='warning' = border-warning/30 bg-warning-surface text-warning) and its docstring states it was built to reuse the semantic tokens so 'tone changes cascade automatically through dark mode and future tenant-theme overrides.'
findings.recommendation: Replace the three degraded banners with <InlineAlert tone="warning"> (+ InlineAlertTitle/Description) and the success Counter tone with 'text-success'. Removes ~30 lines of duplicated palette strings and aligns with the StatusBadge/InlineAlert design-system intent.

findings.title: Webhook-config and Zapier info cards hand-roll blue instead of InlineAlert/info token
findings.evidence: src/components/events/webhook-config-wizard.tsx:223 (Card 'border-blue-200 bg-blue-50/60 dark:border-blue-900 dark:bg-blue-950/40') with :225 'text-blue-600 dark:text-blue-300' icon; identical pattern in src/components/events/zapier-walkthrough.tsx:71 and :73. The --info token (globals.css:177-179, light+dark) and InlineAlert tone='info' (inline-alert.tsx:37) cover this exactly.
findings.recommendation: Replace both informational Card-as-banner blocks with <InlineAlert tone="info"> or, if the Card chrome is wanted, swap the raw blue classes for the info token (border-info/30 bg-info-surface text-info). Keeps the same visual intent while joining the token system.

findings.title: Match-status and quota-effect badges use hardcoded multi-hue palettes — justified deviation, but text-contrast (4.5:1) is unverified
findings.evidence: src/components/events/match-status-badge.tsx:63-87 (5-state ladder: member_contact filled emerald, member_domain outline emerald, member_fuzzy amber, non_member neutral, unmatched destructive) and src/components/events/quota-effect-badge.tsx:52-68 (sky=partnership, violet=cultural, amber=over_quota); same outline-emerald/sky/violet/amber reused for the Partner/Cultural badges in event-detail-header.tsx:163,173 and events-list-table.tsx:162,172. Docstrings (match-status-badge.tsx:56-62) document a U1 verify-finding tuning dark borders -700→-500 for ≥3:1 non-text contrast only.
findings.recommendation: KEEP the hardcoded hues — the 4-token semantic system (success/warning/info/destructive) genuinely cannot express '2 distinct greens' (contact vs domain) or 'sky vs violet' (partnership vs cultural), and color is always paired with icon+text (WCAG 1.4.1 satisfied). Post-launch: (a) verify the badge TEXT colors (text-emerald-900/sky-900/violet-900 on the light tints, and the dark text-*-100 on dark tints) meet 4.5:1 — the documented check only covered the 3:1 non-text border; (b) consider promoting these two confidence/quota palettes into named CSS custom properties (e.g. --match-contact, --quota-partnership) so they still cascade with theming instead of living as inline literals.

findings.title: generateMetadata for erase-PII page uses eventId.slice(0,8) as attendeeName placeholder
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/events/[eventId]/registrations/[registrationId]/erase/page.tsx:48 — `title: t('pageTitle', { attendeeName: eventId.slice(0, 8) })`. The actual registration lookup happens later in the page body (line 128), after `generateMetadata` has already resolved. Browser tab and `<title>` will show 'Erase personal data — a1b2c3d4' (UUID prefix) instead of the attendee's real name.
findings.recommendation: Accept the limitation and update the metadata to a static fallback title that does not expose a UUID fragment: e.g. `t('pageTitleGeneric')` → EN: 'Erase personal data'. Alternatively, load the registration name in `generateMetadata` by calling the use-case (at the cost of a second DB query), which is the pattern used by the member-detail page.

findings.title: 'Not counted' quota badge in attendee table uses HTML title= attribute instead of Radix Tooltip — not keyboard-accessible
findings.evidence: src/components/events/attendee-table.tsx:714 — `<Badge variant='outline' title={tQuotaTip('none')}>`. The tooltip text (`tQuotaTip('none')`) is 'No quota effect — non-member, free ticket, or event is not a partner benefit / cultural event.' and is only exposed via mouse hover through the native browser `title` attribute. `MatchStatusBadge` and `QuotaEffectBadge` for partnership/cultural/over_quota all use Radix `<Tooltip>` with `<TooltipTrigger>` — the 'none' variant breaks the established pattern. WCAG 2.1 SC 1.3.3: relying solely on tooltip text that is only accessible via pointer hover is an advisory issue.
findings.recommendation: Wrap the 'Not counted' badge in a `<Tooltip><TooltipTrigger render={<span className='inline-flex rounded-md' />}>{badge}</TooltipTrigger><TooltipContent>{tQuotaTip('none')}</TooltipContent></Tooltip>` following the exact pattern in quota-effect-badge.tsx. The TooltipProvider is already hoisted at the AttendeeTable root (line 428).

findings.title: Checkmark glyph '✓' in CSV preview table header is hardcoded, not i18n-keyed, and announced verbatim by screen readers
findings.evidence: src/components/events/csv-mapping-form.tsx:952 — `{isCanonical ? '✓ ' : ''}{c}`. The Unicode CHECK MARK character (U+2713) is rendered as visible text content inside a `<th>` element with no `aria-hidden`. Screen readers (NVDA, JAWS) will vocalize this as 'check mark space Basic Info', 'check mark space Status', etc. for every canonical column — producing noisy output for a 6-column header. Additionally, this is a hardcoded presentation bypass of next-intl.
findings.recommendation: Wrap the checkmark with `{'✓ '}`. Alternatively, remove the glyph entirely and rely on the visual emerald accent which already conveys 'canonical column' to sighted users — the sr-only path reads the column name cleanly.

findings.title: CSV preview table header canonical-column accent border (border-b-emerald-500 on emerald-50/70 bg) fails WCAG SC 1.4.11 non-text contrast in light mode
findings.evidence: src/components/events/csv-mapping-form.tsx:935 — `accentClass` for canonical columns includes `border-b-2 border-b-emerald-500 bg-emerald-50/70`. Measured contrast: emerald-500 (#10b981) on emerald-50/70 composite ≈ #ebfaf5 = 2.41:1. WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.11 requires 3:1 minimum for UI component boundaries that convey meaning. The 2px bottom border is the PRIMARY visual indicator that a column drives the import — failure here removes the core 'canonical column' cue in light mode.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade the canonical header accent to `border-b-emerald-600` in light mode (emerald-600 = #059669, contrast ~4.6:1 on emerald-50 bg). Dark mode is unaffected (emerald-500 on dark bg = 7.26:1).

findings.evidence:

` for AT heading-jump navigation (lines 447, 484, 511). The attendee table empty state also emits an `

Add `

findings.title: Events list page inline error card has no role=alert — screen reader users not immediately notified
findings.evidence:
src/app/(staff)/admin/events/page.tsx:211-213 — the use-case failure path renders `

{t('errorState')}

` with no `role='alert'` or `aria-live`. Comparison: the CSV import history page (import/history/page.tsx:82-89) correctly applies `role='alert'` to its error container. The event detail page error card (lines 253-259) also lacks role=alert.
findings.recommendation: Add `role='alert'` to the error container div in both `src/app/(staff)/admin/events/page.tsx:211` and `src/app/(staff)/admin/events/[eventId]/page.tsx:255`. This mirrors the pattern already in use at the import/history error path.

findings.title: Cultural-year quota query: EXTRACT(YEAR … AT TIME ZONE) expression is non-indexable — misleading comment claims index coverage
findings.evidence: src/modules/events/infrastructure/drizzle-registrations-repository.ts:453-470. Comment at line 453-454 states 'uses partial idx events_tenant_cultural_event_idx + per-tenant matched_member idx on event_registrations'. The index events_tenant_cultural_event_idx (schema.ts:106-109) is declared on (tenant_id, is_cultural_event) — a boolean filter. The EXTRACT(YEAR FROM events.start_date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Bangkok') expression at line 470 is a derived expression with no functional index. Postgres cannot push this predicate to the btree; every cultural row that passes the tenant/member/countedAgainst filters must be heap-fetched for the year-check. Recommended EXPLAIN: `EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) SELECT COUNT(*)::int FROM event_registrations er INNER JOIN events e ON e.tenant_id = er.tenant_id AND e.event_id = er.event_id WHERE er.tenant_id = $1 AND er.matched_member_id = $2 AND er.counted_against_cultural_quota = true AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM e.start_date AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Bangkok')::int = $3;`
findings.recommendation: Infrastructure layer (drizzle-registrations-repository.ts). Two options: (a) correct the comment to state the EXTRACT predicate is not index-accelerated and document the MTA scale risk in observability.md § 24 as a known limitation with a LIMIT-based mitigation; or (b) add a functional index `CREATE INDEX event_regs_cultural_year_idx ON event_registrations (tenant_id, matched_member_id, counted_against_cultural_quota) WHERE counted_against_cultural_quota = true;` and accept the year-filter recheck on the small resulting set. Option (a) is the launch-minimal action (no migration needed). Do NOT add a functional index on the EXTRACT expression — Postgres 15 supports expression indexes but they are fragile under TZ definition changes.

findings.title: Fuzzy matcher loads all tenant members into V8 heap with no upper bound — O(M) per attendee, computes Levenshtein in JS
findings.evidence: src/modules/events/infrastructure/drizzle-attendee-matcher.ts:133-137. Query: `executor.select({ memberId, companyName }).from(members).where(eq(members.tenantId, input.tenantId))` — no LIMIT. At match time for each attendee, all M members are fetched, normaliseCompanyName called on each (O(M)), Levenshtein computed per pair (O(M × |name|)). Comment at line 19 documents the design ('fetch the tenant's members (~<2k at design envelope)'). No defensive cap constant exists in the file.
findings.recommendation: Infrastructure layer (drizzle-attendee-matcher.ts). Add a module-level constant MAX_MEMBERS_FOR_FUZZY_MATCH (e.g., 3000) and apply `.limit(MAX_MEMBERS_FOR_FUZZY_MATCH)` to the members SELECT. When rows.length === MAX_MEMBERS_FOR_FUZZY_MATCH emit a structured logger.warn so operators know the fuzzy rule is running at cap (skip the fuzzy pass entirely and fall through to non_member when the cap fires — conservative, avoids false positives on large tenants). This is a single-line Infrastructure change with a constant definition; no Application or Domain changes needed.

findings.title: listPseudonymiseEligible: pseudonymiseEligibilityIdx missing matchType — heap recheck on every row
findings.evidence: src/modules/events/infrastructure/schema.ts:202-204: index is on (tenant_id, registered_at) only. src/modules/events/infrastructure/drizzle-registrations-repository.ts:862-887: query additionally filters matchType IN ('non_member','unmatched') and piiPseudonymisedAt IS NULL, neither of which is in the index. At 50k registrations/tenant with ~5% non-members (~2500 rows), the index returns all rows with registered_at < cutoff (the 2-year window), then heap-fetches each to recheck matchType and piiPseudonymisedAt. Recommended EXPLAIN: `EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) SELECT * FROM event_registrations WHERE tenant_id=$1 AND match_type IN ('non_member','unmatched') AND pii_pseudonymised_at IS NULL AND registered_at < $2 ORDER BY registered_at LIMIT 500;`
findings.recommendation: Infrastructure layer (migration). Add partial index: `CREATE INDEX event_regs_pseudonymise_sweep_idx ON event_registrations (tenant_id, registered_at) WHERE match_type IN ('non_member', 'unmatched') AND pii_pseudonymised_at IS NULL;` This is a Drizzle migration-only change; no code change needed (the drizzle-kit hint comment at schema.ts:346-351 already acknowledges this pattern for other partial indexes). Mark as post-launch since the sweep is not on the latency critical path.

findings.title: updatePaymentStatus uses SELECT-then-UPDATE (2 RTTs) without a merged RETURNING-based pattern — minor TOCTOU window
findings.evidence: src/modules/events/infrastructure/drizzle-registrations-repository.ts:609-656. Pattern: SELECT row to capture previousPaymentStatus (line 609-626), then UPDATE with RETURNING (line 634-644). This opens a TOCTOU window where a concurrent PII erasure between SELECT and UPDATE causes UPDATE to touch 0 rows. The method runs inside the same advisory lock as `maybeApplyStateChange`, so contention from the same-tenant quota path is serialised. The PII erasure path (hardDelete) has its own advisory lock per registration, making a concurrent erase possible.
findings.recommendation: Application layer (drizzle-registrations-repository.ts). For launch: document the TOCTOU window explicitly in the method comment as an accepted known-limitation (it is currently documented only implicitly via the invariant_violation message). Post-launch: rewrite as a single UPDATE ... RETURNING with the previousPaymentStatus captured via a CTE (`WITH prev AS (SELECT payment_status FROM event_registrations WHERE ... FOR UPDATE) UPDATE ... RETURNING prev.payment_status, ...`) to collapse 2 RTTs into 1 and eliminate the window entirely.

findings.title: SLO-F6-005 match-rate gauge computed from audit event scan — gap when eventcreate_audit_fallback_double_failure_total > 0 not documented in observability.md § 24
findings.evidence: src/app/api/internal/observability/recompute-match-rate/route.ts:130-132. The match-rate is computed by scanning `audit_log` for match-resolution events over a rolling 30-day window, then calling `eventcreateMetrics.matchRateGauge(tenantId, value)`. If `eventcreate_audit_fallback_double_failure_total` is non-zero (audit emit failed), those webhook deliveries are invisible to the denominator — the gauge over-reports the match rate. docs/observability.md § 24.2 SLO-F6-005 is listed as 'informational — soft target' but the dependency on audit completeness is not stated.
findings.recommendation: Documentation layer (docs/observability.md § 24.2). Add a note to SLO-F6-005: 'Accuracy depends on audit completeness (SLO-F6-006); when eventcreate_audit_fallback_double_failure_total > 0 the match-rate denominator is understated — investigate SLO-F6-006 before acting on a gauge reading taken during a double-failure window.' No code change required.

findings.title: FR-005 rate-limit spec text (10 req/min) contradicts implemented value (60 req/min) — spec.md not updated after round-2 E13 adjustment
findings.evidence: spec.md:193 FR-005 reads '10 requests per minute per tenant'. src/lib/events-webhook-deps.ts:68 `const F6_WEBHOOK_MAX_PER_MIN = 60`. Route handler comment at src/app/api/webhooks/eventcreate/v1/[tenantSlug]/route.ts:4 says 'Rate-limit check (60 req/min per tenant, FR-005)'. security checklist at specs/012-eventcreate-integration/checklists/security.md:63 documents: 'CHK035 verified: 60 req/min/tenant Upstash rate limit (FR-005 final; round-2 E13 adjustment applied)'. review-20260513-010953.md:146 flags 'spec FR-005 says 10/min — actual implementation 60/min; spec drift to reconcile'.
findings.recommendation: Update spec.md FR-005 to read '60 requests per minute per tenant' and add a parenthetical noting the round-2 E13 upward adjustment from the initial 10 req/min proposal. One-line change; no code change required.

findings.title: drizzle-registrations-repository.ts header comment incorrectly lists implemented methods as stubs
findings.evidence: src/modules/events/infrastructure/drizzle-registrations-repository.ts:16-17 JSDoc lists 'stubs (findByEmailLower, updateMatchAndQuota, listPseudonymiseEligible, pseudonymiseRow, hardDelete) still throw not_implemented'. Verified via grep: updateMatchAndQuota is implemented at line 478, listPseudonymiseEligible at line 856, pseudonymiseRow at line 888, hardDelete at line 947. Only findByEmailLower at line 425-426 is actually still a stub.
findings.recommendation: Update the repository file header comment to remove the four implemented methods and retain only 'findByEmailLower' as the sole remaining stub.

findings.title: reject-broadcast.ts: bare catch swallows all throws as broadcast_concurrent_action_blocked
findings.evidence: src/modules/broadcasts/application/use-cases/reject-broadcast.ts:129 — `catch {}` (no binding, no type narrowing). Any throw from `applyTransition` — Neon connection drop, FK violation, RLS denial, unexpected infra error — is silently swallowed and the caller receives `err({ kind: 'broadcast_concurrent_action_blocked', observedStatus: refresh?.status ?? 'unknown' })` with an HTTP 409. The secondary `findByIdInTx` inside the bare-catch block can itself throw (Neon still down), which would propagate out of the `withTx` callback unhandled, then be caught by the outer catch at line 200 — but only IF `findByIdInTx` throws; if it succeeds, the DB-outage is completely masked as a 409 concurrency error. Approved and cancelled use-cases fixed this exact pattern: approve-broadcast.ts:163 and cancel-broadcast.ts:294 both narrow to `instanceof BroadcastConcurrentMutationError` and re-throw anything else.
findings.recommendation: Narrow the catch to `BroadcastConcurrentMutationError` and re-throw everything else, matching the pattern in approve-broadcast.ts:163. Fix: `catch (e) { if (!(e instanceof BroadcastConcurrentMutationError)) throw e; const refresh = await deps.broadcastsRepo.findByIdInTx(...); return err({ kind: 'broadcast_concurrent_action_blocked', observedStatus: refresh?.status ?? 'unknown' }); }`

findings.title: reconcileStuckSending: BroadcastConcurrentMutationError from markSent surfaces as reconcile.server_error
findings.evidence: src/modules/broadcasts/application/use-cases/reconcile-stuck-sending.ts:219-315 — `markSent` calls `deps.broadcastsRepo.withTx(async (tx) => { await deps.broadcastsRepo.applyTransition(tx, ..., 'sent', ..., 'sending') ... })` with no try/catch around the `withTx` call. If a concurrent webhook already transitioned this broadcast to `sent` between the outer `findById` at line 131 (outside any tx) and the `applyTransition` here, the conditional UPDATE returns 0 rows and `applyTransition` throws `BroadcastConcurrentMutationError`. This propagates out of `markSent`, then is caught by the outer `try/catch` at line 194 in `reconcileStuckSending`, which returns `err({ kind: 'reconcile.server_error', message: e.message })`. The cron handler logs this as a server error and increments an error counter; the admin dashboard shows it as a reconciliation failure.
findings.recommendation: Catch `BroadcastConcurrentMutationError` in `markSent` and return `ok({ kind: 'not_stuck_yet', broadcastId: broadcast.broadcastId, observedStatus: 'already_transitioned' })` instead of re-throwing. Alternatively add a `try/catch` in `reconcileStuckSending` after the call to `markSent` to narrow that specific error. Patch sketch: in `markSent`, wrap the `withTx` call: `try { return await deps.broadcastsRepo.withTx(...); } catch (e) { if (e instanceof BroadcastConcurrentMutationError) return ok({ kind: 'not_stuck_yet', broadcastId: broadcast.broadcastId, observedStatus: 'already_transitioned' }); throw e; }`

findings.title: dispatch-scheduled-broadcast.ts: getMemberPrimaryContact called outside try/catch at Step 2
findings.evidence: src/modules/broadcasts/application/use-cases/dispatch-scheduled-broadcast.ts:459-462 — `const requestingPrimary = await deps.membersBridge.getMemberPrimaryContact(deps.tenant, requestingMember)` is called without a try/catch, outside the Step 1 `withTx` block. If the bridge throws (Neon outage, RLS denial, timeout), the exception propagates to the caller (the cron handler), which does not have a per-broadcast error boundary — it will short-circuit the per-broadcast loop and prevent processing of other approved broadcasts in the same cron tick. The cron-level outer handler should catch this, but the error is not surfaced as a typed `dispatch.server_error` Result — it throws out of `dispatchScheduledBroadcast` entirely, bypassing the `failDispatchAndAudit` cleanup path.
findings.recommendation: Wrap `getMemberPrimaryContact` in a try/catch and route failures through `failDispatchAndAudit` to emit the proper audit trail and return a typed `dispatch.server_error`. Patch: `let requestingPrimary: EmailLower | null; try { requestingPrimary = await deps.membersBridge.getMemberPrimaryContact(...); } catch (e) { await failDispatchAndAudit(deps, input, now, 'member_primary_contact_lookup_failed', 'broadcast_failed_to_dispatch', {...}, 'member_lookup', broadcast); return err({ kind: 'dispatch.server_error', message: ... }); }`

findings.title: customRecipientEmails PII array returned in GET /api/broadcasts/[id] response
findings.evidence: src/app/api/broadcasts/[id]/route.ts:87 — `customRecipientEmails: broadcast.customRecipientEmails` is unconditionally included in the JSON response body. The field persists the full list of per-recipient email addresses supplied at submit time (up to 100 entries). Any session compromise or future IDOR regression would expose this PII batch.
findings.recommendation: Strip `customRecipientEmails` from the GET response; replace with `customRecipientCount: broadcast.customRecipientEmails?.length ?? null`. If the compose UI must allow re-editing the custom list, provide a separate admin-only endpoint or re-validate at submit time. Add a contract test asserting the field is absent from the member-facing GET response body.

findings.title: bodySource stored unsanitised and returned in GET /api/broadcasts/[id] response
findings.evidence: src/app/api/broadcasts/[id]/route.ts:84 — `bodySource: broadcast.bodySource` is returned raw. The field is written at draft/submit time from the client-supplied `bodySource` input, which passes a 200 KB length cap (src/app/api/broadcasts/draft/route.ts:42, src/app/api/broadcasts/submit/route.ts:56) but NO DOMPurify sanitisation (src/modules/broadcasts/application/use-cases/sanitize-html.ts processes only `bodyHtml`, never `bodySource`). The schema stores it directly: src/modules/broadcasts/infrastructure/schema.ts:175. Currently compose-form.tsx:216 sets bodySource=bodyHtml (already client-DOMPurified), but a crafted API call can set bodySource to arbitrary HTML.
findings.recommendation: Server-side: run bodySource through the same DOMPurify sanitiser as bodyHtml at the save-draft and submit-broadcast write boundaries (add a `sanitizeHtml` call on bodySource in save-draft.ts and submit-broadcast.ts before persistence). If bodySource is Tiptap JSON (not HTML), validate it is structurally valid JSON and store it as JSONB, not TEXT. API: remove bodySource from the GET /api/broadcasts/[id] member-facing response; it is editor-internal state that should be fetched separately only when the compose UI re-opens a draft. Add an integration test that POSTs a bodySource containing a script tag and asserts it is either rejected or sanitised before persistence.

findings.title: Inline-image upload draftId not ownership-checked at route layer
findings.evidence: src/app/api/broadcasts/inline-image-upload/route.ts:76 — `draftId` is accepted from the form field and passed directly to the use-case as an audit label (src/modules/broadcasts/application/use-cases/upload-inline-image.ts input.draftId). No DB lookup or ownership assertion is performed at the route or use-case level. Any authenticated member can pass any draftId UUID string.
findings.recommendation: In the route handler, after resolving `ctx.member.memberId`, do a lightweight DB check: `broadcastsRepo.findOwnedByMember(ctx.tenant.slug, ctx.member.memberId, parseBroadcastId(draftId))`. Return 404 on `cross_member` or `not_found` probe kinds. This is the same pattern already used in `snapshot-template-to-draft.ts:123`. Add a contract test asserting that supplying another member's draftId returns 404 and emits `broadcast_cross_member_probe`.

findings.title: Admin banners hardcode emerald/amber/blue palette ladders instead of the existing --success/--warning/--info semantic tokens
findings.evidence: 4 files hardcode Tailwind palette colors with hand-rolled dark overrides, while their own error path correctly uses the `destructive` token: src/components/broadcast/admin/sla-banner.tsx:25-28 (green/amber `border-emerald-200 bg-emerald-50 text-emerald-900 dark:...` + `border-amber-200 bg-amber-50 ...`) and :73-74 (`bg-emerald-200/60 dark:bg-emerald-900/40`, `bg-amber-200/60 dark:bg-amber-900/40`); src/components/broadcast/admin/manager-readonly-banner.tsx:21 (`border-blue-200 bg-blue-50 dark:border-blue-900/50 dark:bg-blue-950/40`) + :24 (`text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300`); src/components/broadcast/admin/batch-breakdown.tsx:290 (`border-amber-200 bg-amber-50 text-amber-900 dark:border-amber-900 dark:bg-amber-950 dark:text-amber-100`); src/components/broadcast/admin/queue-table-client.tsx:154 (`border-amber-400/40 bg-amber-100 text-amber-900 dark:bg-amber-950/40 dark:text-amber-200`). This is substitutable drift, not justified: src/app/globals.css:165-179 + :288-298 define `--success`/`--warning`/`--info` (light AND dark) plus dedicated `--success-surface`/`--warning-surface`/`--info-surface`; globals.css:82-92 maps them to Tailwind (`bg-warning-surface`, `text-info`, etc.); the canonical primitive src/components/ui/inline-alert.tsx:35-39 exposes `tone=success|warning|info|destructive`; and the SAME broadcasts module already consumes these tokens in quota-display.tsx, compose/stale-draft-banner.tsx, clamav-unreachable-banner.tsx (:82-94), and admin/template-library.tsx. The 4 banners are therefore internally inconsistent with their own module.
findings.recommendation: Swap the green/amber/blue branches to the semantic tokens (or to <InlineAlert tone="success|warning|info">) so tone changes cascade through dark mode and future tenant-theme overrides: success → `border-success/30 bg-success-surface text-success`, warning/amber → `border-warning/30 bg-warning-surface text-warning`, info/blue → `border-info/30 bg-info-surface text-info`. The `destructive`/red branch in each file is already correct — leave it. For warning-tone BODY text, follow the module's documented WCAG SC 1.4.11 convention (clamav-unreachable-banner.tsx:82-94, H5 fix 2026-05-21): keep the colored `border-warning` boundary + colored icon, but render body copy in `text-foreground` rather than `text-warning` on a tinted surface, which previously measured ~2.8:1. This is a consistency/maintainability fix, not a launch blocker — current parity works and contrast on the surface-tinted variants is acceptable; route it to the post-launch backlog rather than blocking ship.

findings.title: compose error codes broadcast_custom_recipient_empty and broadcast_custom_recipient_too_many missing from all three locale files
findings.evidence: src/components/broadcast/compose-form.tsx:78-79 — ERROR_CODE_FIELD maps 'broadcast_custom_recipient_empty' and 'broadcast_custom_recipient_too_many' to the 'customList' field, meaning the API can return these codes and the form will attempt tErr(code). en.json portal.broadcasts.compose.errors contains neither key (confirmed: only has broadcast_custom_recipient_unknown and broadcast_custom_recipient_invalid_format). The try/catch fallback at compose-form.tsx:288-292 prevents a crash but silently degrades to either the raw server message string or 'internal_error' — neither of which gives actionable guidance.
findings.recommendation: Add 'broadcast_custom_recipient_empty' and 'broadcast_custom_recipient_too_many' to portal.broadcasts.compose.errors in en.json (e.g. 'Add at least one email address to use custom recipient mode.' and 'Custom list exceeds 100 emails. Remove some entries and try again.'), then add matching keys to th.json and sv.json.

findings.title: ApproveDialog has no pending-state Loader2 spinner on the Confirm button
findings.evidence: src/components/broadcast/admin/approve-dialog.tsx:254-262 — AlertDialogAction only disables (disabled={submitDisabled}) when pending; there is no Loader2 spinner or text change to indicate inflight state. By contrast retry-confirmation-dialog.tsx:191-202 and accept-partial-dialog.tsx:232-243 both show a motion-safe Loader2 + localized 'submitting' text. The inconsistency means an admin clicking Approve sees the button grey-out with no progress indicator during the POST /api/admin/broadcasts/[id]/approve round-trip.
findings.recommendation: Mirror retry-confirmation-dialog.tsx pattern on the AlertDialogAction: add a [pending, startTransition] useTransition-driven conditional that renders <Loader2 className='mr-2 size-4 motion-safe:animate-spin' aria-hidden /> + t('submitting') when pending is true. Add admin.broadcasts.approveDialog.submitting key to all three locale files.

findings.title: SubmitButton (member compose) uses text-only pending state — no Loader2 spinner
findings.evidence: src/components/broadcast/submit-button.tsx:52-62 — the Button renders {submitting ? t('toast.submitting') : t('button.submit')} and sets aria-busy={submitting}, but contains no Loader2 spinner. The 8-second timeout toast (line 38-42) compensates for long waits but provides no sub-8s visual progress signal. The portal.broadcasts.compose.toast.submitting key exists and reads 'Submitting…' but text alone is insufficient per ux-standards.md § 4 (submitting state must be visually distinct beyond colour/text alone for users with cognitive differences).
findings.recommendation: Add <Loader2 className='mr-2 size-4 motion-safe:animate-spin' aria-hidden='true' /> inside the Button when submitting is true, matching the pattern used in accept-partial-dialog.tsx and retry-confirmation-dialog.tsx.

findings.title: getMembersHaltedInTenant called before rate-limit check on every submit — extra RTT on hot path
findings.evidence: src/modules/broadcasts/application/use-cases/submit-broadcast.ts:273-284 — precondition (k) getMembersHaltedInTenant executes before the Upstash rate-limit check at line 287. The comment documents the ordering as 'CHEAP → EXPENSIVE' but places a DB round-trip ahead of the Redis check which is cheaper. getMembersHaltedInTenant calls f3GetMembersHaltedInTenant which issues a Neon query (src/modules/broadcasts/infrastructure/members-bridge.ts:154-170). At SweCham scale (~131 members, <10 halted in normal ops) the query is fast, but adds ~25ms Bangkok RTT on EVERY submit attempt — including the 10/day rate-limited ones that would have been rejected cheaply.
findings.recommendation: Post-launch backlog: swap precondition ordering so rate-limit check (Redis, ~5ms) runs before getMembersHaltedInTenant (Neon, ~25ms). Application layer — submit-broadcast.ts. No schema change required.

findings.title: decideUpgrade uses minTurnoverThb ordering instead of tierBucket enum — allows same-bucket upgrade suggestion
findings.evidence: src/modules/renewals/application/use-cases/evaluate-tier-upgrade.ts:108-118. The filter `candidatesAbove` requires `p.minTurnoverThb > currentPlan.minTurnoverThb` (threshold ordering), NOT a strict `tierBucket` rank comparison. If two plans share the same `tierBucket` but differ in `minTurnoverThb` (e.g. a legacy plan and its successor both map to `'partnership'` tier-bucket), the higher-threshold plan passes the filter and produces a `tier_upgrade_suggested` audit where `from_plan_id` and `to_plan_id` are in the same bucket. The docstring at line 80-84 claims "tier-bucket strictly above", which the code does not enforce. Additionally, `thresholdMetAt = new Date().toISOString()` at line 134 is generated inside a pure decision function, making it wall-clock dependent and non-deterministic under test.
findings.recommendation: 1. Add `tierBucket` rank filter: `candidatesAbove` filter should include `TIER_BUCKET_ORDER[p.tierBucket] > TIER_BUCKET_ORDER[currentPlan.tierBucket ?? 'associate']`. 2. Thread `nowIso` from the outer loop into `decideUpgrade` and replace `new Date().toISOString()` at lines 134/152/164 with the passed value for determinism. The `nowIso` is already available at the call site (`evaluate-tier-upgrade.ts:477`).

findings.title: computeAtRiskScore uses wall-clock `new Date()` for computedAt inside a ClockPort-injected module

The use-case already declares `ClockPort` via `RenewalsDeps`.
findings.recommendation: Replace `computedAt: new Date().toISOString()` at line 196 with `computedAt: deps.clock.now().toISOString()`. One-line fix.

findings.title: drizzle-bounce-event-query step-2 uses global db (intentional, documented)

findings.title: At-risk widget empty-state success icon hardcodes text-emerald-500 instead of text-success token

findings.title: cycle-status-badge hand-rolls a 7-status color map that collapses onto the canonical StatusBadge 5-tone primitive

This deletes the bespoke 14-line dark/light variant table and inherits the AA-tuned surface/foreground tokens. lapsed-tab.tsx:67-94 REASON_VARIANT_CLASSES is the same pattern at lower priority (the red/rose lapsed-vs-payment_failed and orange timed_out severity sub-distinctions are marginal and can map to destructive/warning).

findings.title: Tier / urgency / risk badge multi-hue scales are JUSTIFIED — do not flag as token drift
findings.evidence: src/components/renewals/tier-badge.tsx:22-33 (5 categorical brand hues: thai_alumni=gold/amber, start_up=blue, regular=slate, premium=purple, partnership=emerald), urgency-pill.tsx:16-33 (8-step ordinal gradient slate→amber→orange→red→red+dashed→gray), risk-score-badge.tsx:24-33 (4-band ordinal healthy/warning/at-risk/critical, with orange as a distinct mid-band). All ship explicit dark: variants; docstrings document WCAG dark-ring contrast tuning (-600 floor) and no-color-only signalling per FR-050 (role=img + aria-label).
findings.recommendation: KEEP as-is. The 4-tone semantic system (success/warning/info/destructive) cannot express 5 categorical tiers, an 8-step urgency gradient, or a 4-band scale with a distinct orange mid-band. This is the same justified-deviation pattern as the F6 events match-status confidence ladder. Recording explicitly so a future automated 'hardcoded color' scan does not misclassify these as drift.

findings.title: renewal_cycles.linked_invoice_id has no index — findByInvoiceIdInTx does a table scan
findings.evidence: src/modules/renewals/infrastructure/drizzle/drizzle-renewal-cycle-repo.ts:439-451 implements `findByInvoiceIdInTx` as `WHERE linked_invoice_id = ?` with no ORDER BY or LIMIT-1 push-down beyond `.limit(1)`. Grep of all 38 F8 migrations (0086-0123) finds zero `CREATE INDEX` statements covering `linked_invoice_id`. Existing indexes on renewal_cycles: `pipeline_idx (tenant_id, status, expires_at)`, `eligibility_idx`, `member_idx (tenant_id, member_id)`, `lapsed_tier_idx`. This query is called from: `mark-cycle-complete-from-invoice-paid.ts:123` (F4 invoice-paid callback) and `apply-tier-upgrade-on-paid-callback.ts:199` (tier-upgrade F4 callback).
findings.recommendation: Add a partial index covering the F4 callback hot-path: `CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY renewal_cycles_linked_invoice_idx ON renewal_cycles (tenant_id, linked_invoice_id) WHERE linked_invoice_id IS NOT NULL`. The `IS NOT NULL` partial filter keeps the index small (only cycles that have been linked to an invoice). Run as a standalone migration outside the Drizzle transaction runner (CONCURRENTLY is illegal inside a BEGIN/COMMIT block — precedent in migration 0100 comments). Infrastructure layer.

findings.title: FR-004 quota insights never computed — only 1 of ≥3 specced types fires
findings.evidence: src/modules/insights/application/use-cases/compute-dashboard-snapshot.ts:105 — `candidates` array only ever contains `{ key: 'at_risk_followup', count: atRisk }`. findings.title: FR-001 'indication of unused/under-delivered benefits' absent from dashboard

findings.title: listStuckProcessing uses Date.now() directly, bypassing the injected ClockPort
findings.evidence: src/modules/insights/infrastructure/repos/drizzle-export-job-repo.ts:297 — `const cutoff = new Date(Date.now() - timeoutMs)`. All other time-sensitive paths in this module (processExportJob, downloadExport, prepareExportDownload) receive a ClockPort and call `deps.clock.now()`. The repo constructor receives no clock; this method hardwires the wall-clock.

findings.title: prepareExportDownload expiry check is not atomic with the token mint write
findings.evidence: src/modules/insights/application/use-cases/download-export.ts:105-116 — `findById` (own runInTenant tx, reads expiresAt + status) → expiry check at line 108 → `setDownloadTokenInTx` (separate runInTenant tx at line 113). The guard inside `setDownloadTokenInTx` (drizzle-export-job-repo.ts:226-238) only checks `status IN (ready, delivered)` — it does NOT filter on `expiresAt > now`. Race window: if the cron sweep runs between `findById` returning (job is ready, expiresAt is in the future) and `setDownloadTokenInTx` executing (expiresAt is now past), the token is minted on a TTL-expired row. The sweep also needs a tx; if its `markExpiredInTx` commits before the token-set, the status is `expired`, and the `setDownloadTokenInTx` guard catches it. But if the blob delete succeeds and `markExpiredInTx` races with `setDownloadTokenInTx`, the order is uncertain.

findings.title: No rate limit on member GDPR export request — unbounded job queue growth
findings.evidence: src/app/api/portal/account/data-export/route.ts (entire file) — no rateLimiter import or call. The minute-bucket idempotency key prevents duplicate jobs within the same UTC minute, but a member can create one job per distinct minute → 60 jobs/hour, unlimited over longer windows. Each job triggers `processExportJob` which builds a ZIP (profile, contacts, invoices+PDFs, events, broadcasts, audit subset) and uploads to private Vercel Blob. At SweCham scale this is low-volume, but the pattern has no DB-level cap and the cron worker processes 25 jobs per tick.
findings.recommendation: Add `rateLimiter.check('gdpr-export-request:${memberId}', 3, 3600)` (3 per hour per member) in `POST /api/portal/account/data-export/route.ts` immediately after the session/role gate, following the same pattern used in `/api/admin/audit/export.csv` (lines 67–78). Return 429 with `Retry-After` on limit hit. The admin on-behalf path (`/api/admin/members/[id]/data-export/route.ts`) should add a per-tenant limit as well (`5 per hour per tenant`). Test: add a contract test asserting the 4th request within an hour returns 429.

findings.title: No rate limit on admin directory export request — CPU-intensive PDF build unbounded
findings.evidence: src/app/api/admin/directory/exports/route.ts (entire file) — no rateLimiter import or call. A staff user (admin or manager) can POST `/api/admin/directory/exports` repeatedly to queue unlimited `directory_ebook` (react-pdf render) or `directory_json` jobs. The export job repo has no queue-depth cap.
findings.recommendation: Add `rateLimiter.check('dir-export:${session.user.id}', 5, 300)` (5 per 5 minutes per staff actor) after the session/role gate in `POST /api/admin/directory/exports/route.ts`. Return 429 with `Retry-After`. Implement post-launch as the staff-only risk profile is lower.

findings.title: Download token exposed in Vercel platform access logs via URL query string
findings.evidence: src/app/api/portal/account/data-export/[jobId]/download/route.ts:94 — `url.searchParams.set('token', result.value.token)` then `NextResponse.redirect(url, 303)`. Same pattern in src/app/api/admin/members/[id]/data-export/[jobId]/download/route.ts:64 and src/app/api/admin/directory/exports/[jobId]/download/route.ts:87. The `?token=<value>` appears in Vercel access logs, browser history, and any Referer header from the redirect destination.
findings.recommendation: Post-launch: replace the 303 redirect + query-string pattern with a short-lived (60-second) signed cookie scoped to the proxy path, or pass the token in a `POST` body to the proxy. For the current launch window, document the residual in `specs/015-admin-dashboard/security.md § 5` as an accepted risk with the mitigating controls listed. Verify `EXPORT_DOWNLOAD_TOKEN_SECRET` is rotated and is not shared with any other signing secret.

findings.title: Member logo preview hardcodes bg-white — breaks light/dark parity in dark mode
findings.evidence: src/components/directory/directory-logo-control.tsx:92 — . The logo preview plate hardcodes bg-white. This control renders inside a themed (bg-card) on /portal/profile/directory (portal/profile/directory/page.tsx:54+), which is light/dark via next-themes. In dark mode the white plate is a stark bright rectangle against the dark card surface, breaking visual parity. It is the only hardcoded color across the entire F9 insights presentation surface (dashboard + audit viewer + directory + data-export), and the sole logo-rendering component (no sibling precedent in invoicing to align to), so it is a genuine one-off rather than house style.
findings.recommendation: Replace bg-white with a tokenized neutral plate. If the white plate is intentional for logo legibility (member logos are often designed for white backgrounds and a transparent/light-text logo could vanish on a dark plate), keep a light plate but tokenize it and add a dark-mode-aware treatment — e.g. `bg-background` or `bg-muted`, or `bg-white dark:bg-neutral-200` if a light plate must persist in dark mode. Confirm with product whether member logos require a guaranteed light backdrop; if not, `bg-muted` is the cleanest fix.

findings.title: Thai Buddhist Era calendar not applied on 5 admin date-format instances
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/page.tsx:147 (`asOf` timestamp), :228 (`timeFmt` for activity feed), :245 (`monthFmt` for trend chart x-axis). src/app/(staff)/admin/audit/page.tsx:173 (`dateFmt` for audit log rows). src/app/(staff)/admin/directory/page.tsx:120 (`dateFmt` for export-job timestamps). All five use `new Intl.DateTimeFormat(locale, …)` where `locale` is `'th'` (from `getLocale()`). The project convention documented in `src/lib/format-date-localised.ts` requires `'th-TH-u-ca-buddhist'` for TH locale — the helper `getDateFormatLocale(locale)` encodes this mapping. The `formatLocalisedDate` helper is used correctly on the portal side (`data-export-view-model.ts:47`) but bypassed on all admin pages.
findings.recommendation: In each of the 5 affected sites, replace `new Intl.DateTimeFormat(locale, opts)` with `new Intl.DateTimeFormat(getDateFormatLocale(locale), opts)`. Import `getDateFormatLocale` from `'@/lib/format-date-localised'`. No schema or i18n key changes required — this is display formatting only.

findings.title: ActivityFeedRefresh live-region setTimeout 50ms may under-fire on NVDA
findings.evidence: src/components/dashboard/activity-feed-refresh.tsx:30–31 — `setAnnounced(''); setTimeout(() => setAnnounced(refreshedLabel), 50);`. The clear-then-set pattern is correct for repeat-announce. However NVDA has a ~100ms polling interval for DOM mutations on role=status regions; a 50ms delay means the blank-to-text mutation and the text-to-blank+text mutation may collapse into a single cycle and be dropped by NVDA on a loaded machine. Prior F7.1a US7 review documented `setTimeout(0)` as 'too short for NVDA' and recommended ≥100ms.
findings.recommendation: Change `setTimeout(…, 50)` to `setTimeout(…, 100)` in activity-feed-refresh.tsx:31.

findings.title: Snapshot compute fires 6 separate DB round-trips for member counts (cron hot path)
findings.evidence: src/modules/insights/infrastructure/sources/member-source-adapter.ts:46-59 — `countByStatus` fires 3 parallel `directorySearchWithCount` calls (active, inactive, archived); `countAtRisk` fires 3 more (critical, at-risk, warning). Each `countWith` call invokes `buildMembersDeps(ctx)` + `directorySearchWithCount` + a fresh `runInTenant` transaction with `SET LOCAL app.current_tenant`. The 6 calls are parallelized across `Promise.all` at line 82 of compute-dashboard-snapshot.ts, but each still incurs a Neon Singapore connection + RLS predicate overhead. The spec comment at member-source-adapter.ts:14 acknowledges this and notes '~20k revisit trigger'.
findings.recommendation: For go-live at 5k members this is ACCEPTABLE (within cron budget; cold-start lazy path rare). Document the revisit trigger explicitly in the dashboard runbook. If cold-start latency becomes observable, promote a single-query GROUP BY status aggregate to the members public barrel. Application layer: members barrel use-case. Priority: post-launch-backlog unless cold-start latency is measured to breach SC-002.

findings.title: Invoice-source pagination is O(paid-invoices/100) RTTs — grows linearly with invoice volume
findings.evidence: src/modules/insights/infrastructure/sources/invoice-source-adapter.ts:26-102 — `getYtdPaidRevenueSatang` and `getMonthlyPaidRevenueSatang` both paginate ALL paid invoices in PAGE=100 batches via repeated `listInvoices` calls inside a `do...while(cursor !== null)` loop. Each iteration is a separate `runInTenant` + cursor-keyset query. `countOverdue` does the same over all issued invoices. At 500 invoices (realistic for a 130-member chamber after 3 years): 5 RTTs for YTD + 5 RTTs for monthly trend + N RTTs for overdue = 15+ RTTs before the invoice block completes.
findings.recommendation: Post-launch: promote SQL-level `SUM + GROUP BY month` aggregate queries to the invoicing public barrel. This collapses 3 paginating loops to 2 queries total. Track against the same ~20k-member revisit trigger. Infrastructure layer: invoicing barrel use-case + drizzle-invoice-repo. No action needed for go-live at current scale.

findings.title: export_jobs TTL sweep and stuck-processing scan have no index on time predicates
findings.evidence: drizzle/migrations/0188_f9_export_jobs.sql:66-67 — only `export_jobs_tenant_status_idx (tenant_id, status)` exists. `listSweepable` (drizzle-export-job-repo.ts:265-278) filters on `(tenant_id, status IN ['ready','delivered'], expires_at < NOW())` — the time predicate `expires_at` is not in the index, so the planner does an index scan on (tenant_id, status) then a recheck. `listStuckProcessing` (line 296-309) filters on `(tenant_id, status='processing', updated_at < cutoff)` with the same gap. Both run inside the export cron every ~5 min.
findings.recommendation: Add a partial index in a follow-up migration: `CREATE INDEX export_jobs_sweep_idx ON export_jobs (tenant_id, expires_at) WHERE status IN ('ready','delivered')` and `CREATE INDEX export_jobs_stuck_idx ON export_jobs (tenant_id, updated_at) WHERE status = 'processing'`. Infrastructure layer: a new Drizzle migration. Post-launch-backlog given MVP scale.

findings.title: FR-007a engagement score absent from member profile page

For the dashboard, consider surfacing an engagement distribution chip (e.g., '62% healthy · 18% at-risk') derived from the cached snapshot's `counts` breakdown — or simply accept that the member list serves as the engagement surface for now and scope that as P3.

findings.title: data_export_expired audit event never emitted by sweep cron
