findings.title: search-plans.ts error type leaks raw exception message to server logs
findings.evidence: src/modules/plans/application/search-plans.ts:65-66 — `SearchPlansError = { type: 'server_error'; message: string }` and line 363-367: `return err({ type: 'server_error', message: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e) })`. The route handler at src/app/api/plans/search/route.ts:274-276 logs `err: result.error` via pino, which carries the raw `message` field containing the DB exception string (may include SQL fragments, table/column names).

findings.title: listStuckProcessing uses Date.now() bypassing ClockPort
findings.evidence: src/modules/insights/infrastructure/repos/drizzle-export-job-repo.ts:297 — `const cutoff = new Date(Date.now() - timeoutMs);` inside the Infrastructure repo method. The `ProcessExportJobDeps` interface (process-export-job.ts:56) provides `clock: ClockPort` for all time-dependent logic but the reclaim-stuck path in the repo bypasses it.

findings.title: prepareExportDownload expiry TOCTOU: token minted after expiresAt passes
findings.evidence: The `prepareExportDownload` use-case (wired via src/app/api/portal/account/data-export/[jobId]/download/route.ts:79) calls `findById` in one tx, checks `job.expiresAt > now` in application layer, then calls `setDownloadTokenInTx` in a second tx. `setDownloadTokenInTx` guards `status IN (ready, delivered)` only (src/modules/insights/infrastructure/repos/drizzle-export-job-repo.ts:230-238) — it does NOT check `expiresAt`.
findings.recommendation:
Add `lt(now, exportJobs.expiresAt)` to the WHERE clause in `setDownloadTokenInTx`, or alternatively perform the find + setToken in a single transaction with a `SELECT FOR UPDATE` on the row, checking both `status` and `expiresAt` atomically. Minimal fix:
```ts
// drizzle-export-job-repo.ts setDownloadTokenInTx:
.where(and(
  eq(exportJobs.tenantId, tenantId),
  eq(exportJobs.id, jobId),
  inArray(exportJobs.status, ['ready', 'delivered']),
  gt(exportJobs.expiresAt, new Date()), // add this guard
))
```

findings.title: No rate limit on member GDPR export request (POST /api/portal/account/data-export)
findings.evidence: src/app/api/portal/account/data-export/route.ts:1-107 — no rateLimiter import or call. The idempotency key is scoped to a 1-minute window (requestDataExport use-case), so a member can queue one job per minute indefinitely: 60 jobs/hour × unlimited hours. Each job triggers a blob build (fflate ZIP + react-pdf invoice PDFs + GDPR archive source reads against live Neon). There is no hourly or daily cap per member.
findings.recommendation: Add Upstash rate limit before the `requestDataExport` call: `rateLimiter.check('gdpr-export-request:${memberId}', 3, 3600)` (3 requests per member per hour). Return 429 with `Retry-After: 3600` on limit exceeded. Emit a `gdpr_export_rate_limited` audit event (best-effort). Add a contract test asserting the 4th request within 1 hour returns 429.

findings.title: raw DB e.message leaks into Vercel function logs via SearchPlansError
findings.evidence: src/modules/plans/application/search-plans.ts:363-368 builds `SearchPlansError = { type: 'server_error', message: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e) }`. src/app/api/plans/search/route.ts:274-276 passes this plain object as `{ err: result.error }` to pino. Because pino is configured with no `serializers.err` but `SearchPlansError` is a plain object with enumerable fields, its `message` property (containing the raw Postgres exception text, which can include table names, column names, and SQL fragments) is serialised into Vercel function logs.
findings.recommendation: In src/modules/plans/application/search-plans.ts:363-368, replace `message: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)` with `errKind: errKind(e)` (import from @/lib/log-id). Update src/app/api/plans/search/route.ts:275 to log `{ requestId: ctx.requestId, errKind: result.error.errKind }` instead of `err: result.error`. This matches the errKind-only pattern already used throughout F9 and F5.

findings.title: No rate limit on staff directory export enqueue (POST /api/admin/directory/exports)
findings.evidence: src/app/api/admin/directory/exports/route.ts:29-79 — no rateLimiter import or call. Admin and manager roles can queue unlimited directory_ebook (react-pdf, CPU-heavy for large directories) or directory_json jobs. Each job builds a private Blob artefact processed by the cron worker.
findings.recommendation: Add `rateLimiter.check('dir-export:${tenantId}', 5, 300)` (5 directory export requests per tenant per 5 minutes). Return 429 on limit exceeded. Acceptable to defer to post-launch backlog given the staff-only gate.

findings.title: No rate limit on admin on-behalf GDPR export (POST /api/admin/members/[id]/data-export)
findings.evidence: src/app/api/admin/members/[id]/data-export/route.ts:22-73 — no rateLimiter import or call. An admin can queue GDPR archive builds for any member in the tenant without a per-actor or per-tenant frequency cap.
findings.recommendation: Add `rateLimiter.check('admin-gdpr-export:${tenantId}', 10, 3600)` (10 on-behalf exports per tenant per hour). Acceptable to defer to post-launch backlog.

findings.title: ActivityFeedRefresh live region announced at 50ms — may miss NVDA's refresh cycle

findings.title: listSweepable and listStuckProcessing in drizzle-export-job-repo.ts filter on time columns not covered by the existing (tenant_id, status) index
findings.evidence: src/modules/insights/infrastructure/repos/drizzle-export-job-repo.ts:265-279 (listSweepable): filters `status IN ('ready','delivered') AND expires_at < NOW()`. Migration 0188_f9_export_jobs.sql line 66 creates `export_jobs_tenant_status_idx ON (tenant_id, status)` — the `expires_at` predicate is not covered. Lines 296-311 (listStuckProcessing): filters `status = 'processing' AND updated_at < $cutoff` — `updated_at` also not indexed. Both queries must recheck the time predicate via Bitmap Heap Scan after the status index narrows rows.
findings.recommendation: Infrastructure layer (migrations): add a partial covering index: `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS export_jobs_tenant_sweepable_idx ON export_jobs (tenant_id, expires_at) WHERE status IN ('ready', 'delivered')` and `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS export_jobs_tenant_stuck_idx ON export_jobs (tenant_id, updated_at) WHERE status = 'processing'`. findings.title: directory search query uses ILIKE on members.company_name, directory_listings.industry, and description — no pg_trgm index on listings columns

findings.title: listSmartInsights is called in the dashboard page AFTER the snapshot result is already available — double dismissal-check RTT on cache-warm paths

findings.title: Raw attendee email stored in audit_log payload for attendee_non_member events
findings.evidence: src/modules/events/application/use-cases/_helpers/process-attendee-in-tx.ts:467 — `attendeeEmail: asAttendeeEmail(attendeeEmail)` is the full raw email address written into the `attendee_non_member` audit event payload. The audit_log row has a 5-year retention_years default. The F6 AuditPayloads type at src/modules/events/application/ports/audit-port.ts:335 declares `readonly attendeeEmail: AttendeeEmail` for this event type, confirming the contract intentionally carries the full email.
findings.recommendation: Replace the full email with a SHA-256 hex prefix (16 chars) identical to the pattern already in use for CSV import rows. At process-attendee-in-tx.ts, apply `hashAttendeeEmail(attendeeEmail)` (the same helper defined at import-csv.ts:387) and rename the payload field to `attendeeEmailHash`. Update the AuditPayloads type at audit-port.ts:332-336 to `readonly attendeeEmailHash: string`. Add 'attendeeEmailHash' to the GLOBAL_SENSITIVE_PAYLOAD_FIELDS deny-list in audit-redaction.ts as a precaution. Also apply the same fix to `attendee_matched_member_contact` at line ~426 where `matchedOnEmail: asAttendeeEmail(attendeeEmail)` stores the full matched contact email — replace with a domain-only value or a hash prefix. Framework: GDPR Art. 5(1)(c), Art. 25(1) data protection by design; PDPA §19.

findings.title: GDPR portability archive (Art. 20) omits material personal-data fields from profile projection
findings.evidence: src/modules/insights/infrastructure/sources/gdpr-archive-source-adapter.ts:270-286 — the profile object serialised into profile.json includes only: memberId, companyName, legalEntityType, country, taxId, website, description, foundedYear, planId, planYear, status, registrationDate, registrationFeePaid, createdAt, updatedAt. It omits fields that ARE personal data stored on the same members row: notes (admin free-text, schema-members.ts:83), turnoverThb (financial metric, schema-members.ts:61), addressLine1/addressLine2/city/province/postalCode (address fields added in migration 0195, schema-members.ts:86-93), riskScoreFactors (jsonb, schema-members.ts:143), blockedFromAutoReactivationReason (text, schema-members.ts:158), and renewalRemindersOptedOut (preference flag). The contacts projection at lines 287-299 also omits dateOfBirth collected for Thai Alumni.
findings.recommendation: Add all omitted fields to the profile projection: notes, turnoverThb, addressLine1, addressLine2, city, province, postalCode. For risk fields: include riskScore, riskScoreBand, riskScoreFactors, riskSnoozedUntil (these are computed about the member and are their personal data). For F8 fields: include renewalRemindersOptedOut, renewalRemindersOptedOutAt, blockedFromAutoReactivation, blockedFromAutoReactivationReason. Add dateOfBirth to the contacts projection. Notes caveat: the archive README should label notes as 'admin-entered field (may contain operational annotations)' so the member understands the provenance. For fields that could carry third-party annotations (notes), the legitimate-interest framing for admin free-text vs the member's own right-of-access needs DPO sign-off on the disclosure wording, but the field itself must be included. Framework: GDPR Art. 20(1); PDPA §30 portability right.

findings.title: attendee_matched_member_contact audit event stores full raw matched-contact email in payload
findings.evidence: src/modules/events/application/use-cases/_helpers/process-attendee-in-tx.ts:426 — `matchedOnEmail: asAttendeeEmail(attendeeEmail)` is the full raw email address written into the `attendee_matched_member_contact` audit event payload. The AuditPayloads type at src/modules/events/application/ports/audit-port.ts:319 declares `readonly matchedOnEmail: AttendeeEmail`. This persists the exact contact email that the attendee matched on, for 5 years in audit_log.
findings.recommendation: Replace `matchedOnEmail: asAttendeeEmail(attendeeEmail)` with the email domain only: `matchedOnEmailDomain: attendeeEmail.split('@')[1] ?? ''`. This is enough to confirm the match type (domain match vs exact contact match) without exposing the full email. Update the AuditPayloads type at audit-port.ts:319 accordingly. Add 'matchedOnEmail' to the GLOBAL_SENSITIVE_PAYLOAD_FIELDS deny-list in audit-redaction.ts to protect any existing rows in viewers. Framework: GDPR Art. 5(1)(c), Art. 25(1); PDPA §19.

findings.title: ESLint flat-config shadowing silently disables drizzle-orm + infra-value import bans for members and invoicing application layers
findings.evidence: eslint.config.mjs:455-474 (members-specific no-restricted-imports block) and :433-452 (invoicing-specific block) are later flat-config entries that SHADOW the application-layer no-restricted-imports rule at :136-203. In ESLint flat-config, the last matching rule for a given rule-name wins. Result: `pnpm lint` exits clean on src/modules/members/application/use-cases/count-active-members-on-plan.ts:25 (drizzle-orm value import) and src/modules/invoicing/application/invoicing-deps.ts:13-32 (multiple infra-adapter value imports). Confirmed by running `pnpm exec eslint` with a standalone drizzle-orm rule on count-active-members-on-plan.ts — it errors; the project config does not.
findings.recommendation: Consolidate the members and invoicing `no-restricted-imports` blocks by merging their `patterns` arrays INTO the existing application-layer block (lines 136-203) using the `files` selector restricted to the specific module paths, OR add the bidirectional port patterns as additional `patterns` entries inside the existing application-layer block. Run `pnpm exec eslint src/modules/members/application/use-cases/count-active-members-on-plan.ts` after the fix to confirm the drizzle-orm import is now flagged. Also add a CI sanity-smoke: `pnpm exec eslint --rule '{"no-restricted-imports":["error",{"paths":[{"name":"drizzle-orm"}]}]}' src/modules/*/application/**/*.ts` to guard against future regressions.

findings.title: Application use case imports drizzle-orm operators and schema table directly — count-active-members-on-plan.ts

findings.title: Auth application use cases import infrastructure VALUES (not just types): MalformedHashError and retryAfterSeconds
findings.evidence: src/modules/auth/application/change-password.ts:44 `import { MalformedHashError } from '@/modules/auth/infrastructure/password/argon2-hasher';` and :46 `import { retryAfterSeconds } from '@/modules/auth/infrastructure/rate-limit/upstash-rate-limiter';`. findings.title: GDPR archive audit-event subset uses manager projection — blocks F6 event email correlation for the subject themselves
findings.evidence: src/modules/insights/application/gdpr-audit-subset.ts:115-116 — `redactSummaryForRole(r.summary, 'manager')` and `redactPayloadForRole(r.eventType, r.payload, 'manager')` apply the manager deny-list to the member's own audit entries. The GLOBAL_SENSITIVE_PAYLOAD_FIELDS deny-list at audit-redaction.ts:55 includes 'email'. The P0 fix (replacing raw email in attendee_non_member with a hash) will also affect what the subject sees in their own archive. More immediately: if the P0/P1 fix is applied and the field is renamed to attendeeEmailHash, the subject's archive will correctly show the hash. But the current manager projection strips any field named 'email' — which means the subject cannot see their OWN email in any audit event payload that happens to use that field name.
findings.recommendation: Consider creating a dedicated 'subject-self' projection role that retains the subject's own actor_user_id-linked emails but still strips third-party emails from targeted events. Alternatively, document the current approach in gdpr-audit-subset.ts as an intentional conservative design choice and accept the over-redaction as a tolerable limitation disclosed in the archive README ('Some email fields in operational audit events have been redacted to protect third-party privacy; your email address is available in the contacts section of this archive'). If no change is made to the code, update the GDPR archive README template to explain the redaction. Framework: GDPR Art. 20(1), Recital 63.

findings.title: export_jobs table has no documented retention period for failed/expired jobs with member PII in subjectMemberId
findings.evidence: src/modules/insights/infrastructure/db/schema-insights.ts:122-156 — the export_jobs table has status enum values including 'failed' and 'expired'. The TTL sweep at src/app/api/cron/insights/process-export-jobs/route.ts:75-90 deletes the private Blob artefact and transitions the row to 'expired', but does NOT delete the export_jobs row itself. The row carries subjectMemberId (a UUID identifying the member) and requestedBy (the actor user UUID). There is no scheduled job or migration to hard-delete expired/failed export_jobs rows. GDPR Art. 5(1)(e) (storage limitation) requires that data not be retained longer than necessary.
findings.recommendation: Add a scheduled sweep that hard-deletes export_jobs rows where status IN ('expired', 'failed') AND updated_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '90 days'. 90 days gives sufficient time for incident investigation while respecting storage limitation. Document this retention period in the RoPA. Add the sweep to the existing process-export-jobs cron (step 4) or create a separate monthly cron. Framework: GDPR Art. 5(1)(e); PDPA §19 purpose limitation.

findings.title: acknowledgeBroadcastsTerms audit emit failure leaves consent recorded in F3 column but unaudited — no backfill mechanism exists
findings.evidence: src/modules/broadcasts/application/use-cases/acknowledge-broadcasts-terms.ts:142-171 — the member_acknowledged_broadcasts_terms audit event is emitted in a try/catch that swallows the failure to logger.error. The comment at line 142 acknowledges 'a future audit-row-backfill cron can recover from logger entries if needed.' No such cron exists in the codebase.
findings.recommendation: Implement the acknowledged 'future backfill cron' or, better, change the consent recording to use a single atomic transaction: emit the F7 audit row inside the same transaction as the F3 column update (requires exposing a transactional audit.emit path from F7's audit port that the F3 bridge can accept). If atomicity is not achievable across module boundaries, add a compensating cron that queries members WHERE broadcasts_acknowledged_at IS NOT NULL AND the audit_log has no member_acknowledged_broadcasts_terms row for that member_id, then inserts the missing rows. Framework: GDPR Art. 7(1) demonstrability; PDPA §24 consent record.

findings.title: GDPR archive notes field carries admin internal annotations — needs README disclosure
findings.evidence: src/modules/members/infrastructure/db/schema-members.ts:82-83 — notes column is 'Admin-only free text — redacted from member-self GET responses in Application.' The archive-member use-case at src/modules/members/application/use-cases/archive-member.ts:204 documents 'Flagged for F9 GDPR self-service export carve-out alongside notes and override_reason_note'. Once the P1 fix adds notes to the GDPR archive profile projection, the member will see admin-entered operational notes that may include staff judgements about their account.
findings.recommendation: Update the GDPR archive README template (src/modules/insights/infrastructure/sources/gdpr-readme.ts) to include a section explaining the notes field: 'The notes field contains operational annotations entered by chamber staff regarding your membership account.' Also ensure that the override_reason_note from the archive-member use-case is included in the archive (it currently references this gap). DPO should review whether notes qualify as 'controller-internal' data that may be withheld under Art. 20(3) or whether they are 'data concerning the data subject' that must be disclosed under Art. 15/20.

findings.title: member_updated audit payload stores diff with raw old/new values including sensitive fields (taxId, turnoverThb, notes, address)
findings.evidence: src/modules/members/application/use-cases/update-member.ts:86-98 buildDiff function captures full old and new values for every changed field. The audit payload at line 210-215 contains `{ member_id, fields_changed, diff }` where diff carries `{ old: currentVal, new: patch[key] }` for fields like taxId, turnoverThb, notes, addressLine1, etc. The member_updated event's diff field is already in the SENSITIVE_PAYLOAD_FIELDS deny-list at audit-redaction.ts:104 so it is stripped from manager views — but it remains in the full admin view and in the GDPR archive audit-events.json (via the manager projection, which does strip it).
findings.recommendation: For the most sensitive fields (taxId, notes), replace the raw old/new diff value with a change-indicator only: `{ old: '[redacted]', new: '[redacted]', changed: true }`. The fields_changed list already provides accountability (which fields changed); the raw values are rarely needed for operational forensics. Alternatively, store only a SHA-256 hash of the old and new values for taxId. For the GDPR archive context, the manager projection already redacts the diff — no archive change needed. The change would affect admin-only audit views. Framework: GDPR Art. 5(1)(c); PDPA §19.

findings.title: No documented retention enforcement for member and contact rows after archival
findings.evidence: src/modules/members/domain/member.ts:174 — ARCHIVE_UNDELETE_WINDOW_DAYS = 90 (the undelete window). After 90 days an archived member cannot be undeleted, but there is no code in any module that hard-deletes member or contact rows after the archival + retention period expires. The schema-members.ts and schema-contacts.ts have no deleted_at-based sweep cron. The F6 pseudonymise-stale-non-member-pii.ts sweeps non-member event registrations after 2 years — a pattern that does not exist for members.
findings.recommendation: Define and document a retention schedule for member/contact rows: 'Archived members: retain for 5 years post-archival (Thai Revenue Code §87/3 obligation covers active period + 5 years), then pseudonymise companyName, contactName, email, phone, taxId, address fields. Notes and riskScoreFactors: delete at pseudonymisation. Audit log rows referencing the member: pseudonymise actor/target identifiers per existing F6 pattern.' Implement a scheduled cron (annual, low-volume) that pseudonymises archived members older than 5 years. Framework: GDPR Art. 5(1)(e), Art. 17(3)(b); PDPA §19; Thai Revenue Code §87/3.

findings.title: Broadcasts consent banner: acknowledgement stores locale but not consent notice version
findings.evidence: src/modules/broadcasts/application/use-cases/acknowledge-broadcasts-terms.ts:147-155 — the member_acknowledged_broadcasts_terms audit payload stores memberId, userId, acknowledgedAt, bannerLocale. It does not store a consent notice version or URL. The members.broadcasts_acknowledged_at column (schema-members.ts:116) similarly has no associated version column. The banner content lives in i18n messages, which may change over time.
findings.recommendation: Add a notice_version field to the audit payload (e.g. a short hash of the banner text at time of acknowledgement, or a semantic version string maintained in i18n config). Add a broadcasts_terms_notice_version text column to the members table. This provides a durable record linking the acknowledgement to the specific notice text the member saw. If the notice version is not implementable for go-live, at minimum document the current banner version in the deployment runbook so it can be manually correlated. Framework: GDPR Art. 7(1); PDPA §24.

findings.title: Auth application files import TenantSlug and EmailLocale via deep paths instead of the modules' public barrels
findings.evidence: src/modules/auth/application/create-user.ts:69 `import type { TenantSlug } from '@/modules/tenants/domain/tenant-slug';` — TenantSlug is exported at src/modules/tenants/index.ts:30. src/modules/auth/application/reissue-invitation.ts:67 same pattern. src/modules/members/application/ports/reissue-invitation-port.ts:27 and src/modules/members/application/use-cases/invite-portal.ts:28 same. Additionally: src/modules/auth/application/create-user.ts:61, forgot-password.ts:45-48, reissue-invitation.ts:63 import `EmailLocale` from `@/modules/auth/infrastructure/email/reset-password-email` directly — EmailLocale is re-exported from the auth barrel at index.ts:185 and is a type-only alias (`'en' | 'th' | 'sv'`). All are `import type` so there is no runtime impact.
findings.recommendation: 1. Change all `from '@/modules/tenants/domain/tenant-slug'` to `from '@/modules/tenants'` in the four auth/members application files. 2. Change all `from '@/modules/auth/infrastructure/email/reset-password-email'` type imports of EmailLocale to `from '@/modules/auth'` (the barrel re-export). These are `import type` changes — no behaviour change, no test run required beyond `pnpm typecheck`.

findings.title: Audit-log event-type filter dropdown omits ~80% of event types that actually appear in the log (member/invoice/broadcast/renewal/plan/event)
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/audit/page.tsx:44-47 builds EVENT_TYPE_OPTIONS = [...AUDIT_EVENT_TYPES, ...F9_AUDIT_EVENT_TYPES]. AUDIT_EVENT_TYPES (src/modules/auth/domain/audit-event.ts:36-101) is ONLY ~36 auth + Stripe-webhook codes; F9_AUDIT_EVENT_TYPES (src/modules/insights/application/ports/audit-port.ts:36-48) is ~7 F9 read codes. The audit_log table holds 200+ event types across F2-F8 (audit.eventType i18n catalogue alone lists 99: member_*=15, invoice/credit/receipt_*=14, renewal_*=25, plan_*, etc.). The filter is a CLOSED shadcn <Select> with no free-text fallback (src/components/audit/audit-filters.tsx:117-142, comment says '~44 event-type codes'). The backend imposes NO restriction — auditQuery accepts an open eventType?: readonly string[] (src/modules/insights/application/use-cases/audit-query.ts:56), so the only limit is the UI option list.
findings.recommendation: Populate EVENT_TYPE_OPTIONS from the full union of every module's audit event-type catalogue (plans/members/invoicing/payments/broadcasts/renewals/events + auth + F9), e.g. the same set already enumerated in audit.eventType i18n keys, deduped + sorted. Backend already supports it; this is a one-list fix. The auditEventCategory grouping in audit-filters.tsx already exists to keep a long list navigable.

findings.title: Dashboard 'At-risk members' count (3 bands) drills into a members list filtered to only 1 band — count vs destination mismatch, critical members hidden from drill-down
findings.evidence: Dashboard at-risk KPI + needs-attention count come from countAtRisk which sums THREE bands: AT_RISK_BANDS = ['critical','at-risk','warning'] (src/modules/insights/infrastructure/sources/member-source-adapter.ts:28,55-57). The needs-attention link goes to /admin/members?risk_band=at-risk (src/app/(staff)/admin/page.tsx:200). The members page maps risk_band to a SINGLE-band equality filter: eq(members.riskScoreBand, filter.riskBand) (src/modules/members/infrastructure/db/drizzle-member-repo.ts:147-148), and the filter UI is a single-value <Select> (src/components/members/directory-filters.tsx:185, RISK_BANDS single-select). There is no multi-band filter value.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) change the needs-attention/KPI link to a filter that matches the 3-band count (e.g. add a multi-band query param risk_band=warning,at-risk,critical or a single 'needs-attention' band group, and teach the members page + filter to accept it), or (b) narrow countAtRisk to the single 'at-risk' band so count and drill-down agree. Option (a) preserves surfacing critical members.

findings.title: Invoice 'Overdue' status filter returns zero rows — overdue is read-time-derived, never a stored status, so eq(status,'overdue') matches nothing
findings.evidence: The invoices filter dropdown offers 'overdue' as a selectable status (src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/_components/invoice-filters.tsx:35-43 STATUS_VALUES includes 'overdue'; page VALID_STATUSES also includes it, page.tsx:43-51). But 'overdue' is NOT a DB enum value: invoiceStatusEnum = draft|issued|paid|void|credited|partially_credited (src/modules/invoicing/infrastructure/db/schema-invoices.ts:29-36); it is computed at read-time via computeIsOverdue (page.tsx:264-268). listInvoicesPaged is called WITHOUT zod validation (use-case does not .parse(), src/modules/invoicing/application/use-cases/list-invoices.ts:77-92), so status:'overdue' flows to the repo which does filters.push(eq(invoices.status, opts.status)) (drizzle-invoice-repo.ts:412-413) — matching zero rows.
findings.recommendation: Handle 'overdue' specially in the page/repo: when status==='overdue', filter to status='issued' AND due_date < tenant-today (the same rule computeIsOverdue applies), instead of equality on the stored enum. Or remove 'overdue' from the selectable STATUS_VALUES if not supported.

findings.title: Member-detail write affordances (Edit/Archive/Add-Contact/Invite/Promote/Remove) are shown to the read-only manager and dead-end at the API
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/members/[memberId]/page.tsx — action buttons are gated only by member.status, never by role: 'Edit' link (lines 576-582), ArchiveMemberButton (572-575), 'Add contact' ContactFormDialog (782-798), per-contact ContactActions (335-350), InvitePortalButton (325-327), ResendBouncedInviteButton (332-334). Compare the SAME page's invoice section (member-invoices-section.tsx:104-133, 385-439) which renders managers a disabled button + tooltip ('disabledForManager'). The detail page never receives or checks session.user.role for these member-level actions. Server-side the writes ARE blocked: archive route uses requireAdminContext (src/app/api/members/[memberId]/archive/route.ts:42-46 → 403 'forbidden'), contacts route same (src/app/api/members/[memberId]/contacts/route.ts:31-35), and the Edit page hard-404s managers (src/app/(staff)/admin/members/[memberId]/edit/page.tsx:67 `if (user.role !== 'admin') notFound()`).
findings.recommendation: Thread session.user.role into the member detail page (it already calls requireSession('staff') at line 394 — just pass role down). Mirror the proven member-invoices-section pattern: for role==='manager' either omit the member-level write CTAs entirely (consistent with the list page hiding 'Add member') OR render them disabled with the existing admin.members.invoices.actions.disabledForManager tooltip. At minimum gate the PageHeader Edit/Archive block, the Contacts 'Add' button, and the ContactActions/Invite/Resend clusters on role==='admin'. The i18n keys already exist (disabledForManager in en/th/sv:1131), so no new translations are needed for the disabled-tooltip approach.

findings.title: Renewal-reminder email CTA points to /portal/account (settings), not the renewal flow — the step's primary action dead-ends
findings.evidence: src/modules/renewals/application/use-cases/_lib/dispatch-one-cycle.ts:774 and src/modules/renewals/application/use-cases/retry-failed-reminders.ts:205 both set `renewal_link_url: \`${env.app.baseUrl}/portal/account\``. /portal/account (src/app/(member)/portal/account/page.tsx) is the password/locale/data-export settings page and contains NO link to the renewal flow. So a member who clicks 'Renew now' in the reminder lands on a settings page with no path forward to actually renew.
findings.recommendation: Point the email CTA at the signed-token redeem-link route that was built for exactly this: `${env.app.baseUrl}/api/portal/renewal/redeem-link?t=<signedToken>`. Generate the HMAC token via the existing renewalLinkTokenSigner per (tenant, member, cycle) and interpolate it into renewal_link_url at dispatch time. The dispatch comment at lines 770-773 already flags /portal/account as a stopgap 'until the signed-token renewal route lands' — that route HAS landed (src/app/api/portal/renewal/redeem-link/route.ts).

findings.title: Signed-token renewal redeem-link route + HMAC signer are dead code — no caller constructs the token URL
findings.evidence: Grep for `renewal_link_url` across src returns only the two dispatch call-sites (both hardcode /portal/account) plus the gateway pass-through (resend-transactional-renewal-gateway.tsx:289). Nothing builds a `redeem-link?t=` URL. src/app/api/portal/renewal/redeem-link/route.ts (verifies HMAC, mints session, redirects to /portal/renewal/[memberId]) and src/modules/renewals/infrastructure/renewal-link-token/hmac-signer.ts therefore have zero production callers. The renewal page (src/app/(member)/portal/renewal/[memberId]/page.tsx:37-43) takes only `params`, no `searchParams`, so it cannot even read a `?token=` even though the email-template JSDoc claims the URL carries one (renewal-reminder-email.tsx:22).
findings.recommendation: Wire the dispatch use-cases to sign a token and emit the redeem-link URL (see finding above). This single change activates the dormant route and signer. Add an integration test asserting the dispatched email's CTA href matches `/api/portal/renewal/redeem-link?t=`.

findings.title: Renewal flow (/portal/renewal/[memberId]) and reminder opt-out (/portal/preferences/renewals) have zero inbound UI links — unreachable except by direct URL
findings.evidence: memberNavConfig (src/config/nav.ts:244-303) has Dashboard, Profile, Invoices, Benefits, Broadcasts, Timeline, Account — no Renewal and no Preferences entry. Grep for `portal/renewal/` and `portal/preferences/renewals` across src/app/**/*.tsx finds only the pages' own files and route helpers — no `<Link href>` from any other page. /portal/account (account/page.tsx) links to data-export but not to renewal preferences. The portal dashboard (portal/page.tsx) shows no 'expiring soon / renew now' surface.
findings.recommendation: Add an in-app renewal entry point: surface an 'expiring soon → Renew' banner/card on /portal dashboard and/or the profile Plan card when an active renewal cycle exists, linking to /portal/renewal/[memberId]. Add a link to /portal/preferences/renewals from the Account page (next to the data-export card) and from the reminder email footer.

findings.title: Renewal reminder email has no opt-out/unsubscribe link, yet an opt-out page exists (FR-016)
findings.evidence: src/modules/renewals/infrastructure/email/templates/base-renewal-layout.tsx:104-110 renders only a CTA button + footer brand line — no opt-out/manage-preferences link. The opt-out toggle page /portal/preferences/renewals (FR-016) exists but is referenced nowhere in the email or UI (Grep results above).
findings.recommendation: Add a localised 'Manage reminder preferences' / opt-out link in the reminder email footer pointing to /portal/preferences/renewals (ideally token-authenticated like the unsubscribe flow does in src/app/unsubscribe/[token]/page.tsx). Mirror the F7 broadcast pattern which has a robust signed-token unsubscribe surface.

findings.title: Profile Plan card omits the plan/tier name — 'view own plan' shows only a year
findings.evidence: src/app/(member)/portal/profile/page.tsx:184-219 renders the Plan card with only planYear, registrationDate, and status. It never renders the plan name or tier even though `getMember` resolves the full member and the entity carries `planId` (src/modules/members/domain/member.ts:151). The human-readable plan/tier is only shown on the renewal page (renewal/[memberId]/page.tsx:192-195, unreachable via nav) and tier benefits only on /portal/benefits.
findings.recommendation: Resolve planId → plan name + tier (via F2 listPlans/getPlan or a plans port) in profile/page.tsx and render it in the Plan card; add a 'View benefits' link from the Plan card to /portal/benefits so plan identity and entitlements are connected.

findings.title: Dashboard 'Overdue invoices' link points to ?status=issued (all issued), not the overdue subset — count and destination disagree
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/page.tsx:192-197 renders the overdueInvoices needs-attention item with count metrics.needsAttention.overdueInvoices (from invoiceSource.countOverdue) but href '/admin/invoices?status=issued'. status=issued shows ALL issued invoices including not-yet-due ones, a superset of the overdue count. (This is the workaround for the broken ?status=overdue filter above.)
findings.recommendation: Once the 'overdue' filter is fixed (finding above), change the link to /admin/invoices?status=overdue so the destination filters to exactly the overdue set the count represents.

findings.title: 'Record payment' link from member detail uses #payment anchor that doesn't exist on the invoice detail page
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/members/[memberId]/_components/member-invoices-section.tsx:388 links to `/admin/invoices/${inv.invoiceId}#payment`. The invoice detail page (src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/[invoiceId]/page.tsx) has NO element with id="payment"; the record-payment action is a RecordPaymentDialog trigger button in the PageHeader actions (line 344-348). Grep for id="payment"/#payment scrollIntoView in the [invoiceId] subtree returns no match. (The page DOES have id="payment-details-heading" at line 533 but only when status==='paid', and the fragment is '#payment' not '#payment-details-heading'.)
findings.recommendation: Either point the link at the plain invoice detail (#payment removed) since the Record Payment button is already prominent in the header, OR add an id="payment" anchor + auto-open the RecordPaymentDialog when the hash is present (a client effect reading location.hash). Prefer the latter to honor the original intent.

findings.title: New-invoice member picker excludes 'inactive' members (loads status:['active'] only), blocking a billing path the domain allows
findings.evidence: src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/new/page.tsx:85-88 loads the member dropdown with status:['active'] only. The form honors ?memberId= deep-link ONLY if it is in that list, else silently resets to '' (src/app/(staff)/admin/invoices/_components/invoice-form.tsx:75-80). The form comment (lines 62-66) claims only 'archived' members are excluded — but inactive are excluded too. Yet the domain PERMITS inactive members: create-invoice-draft.ts:128 and issue-invoice.ts:208 reject ONLY member.isArchived ({code:'member_archived'}); inactive is allowed.
findings.recommendation: Load the picker with status:['active','inactive'] (keep archived excluded to match the domain guard), and/or, when ?memberId= resolves to a member not in the list, fetch/show that single member as a pre-selected option with a note rather than silently clearing it.

findings.title: Smart-insight 'at_risk_followup' line is informational text with a scopeRef but no link to act on it
findings.evidence: computeDashboardSnapshot emits at_risk_followup with a count (src/modules/insights/application/use-cases/compute-dashboard-snapshot.ts:105) and the page threads scopeRef into the InsightLine (src/app/(staff)/admin/page.tsx:222-226). But InsightsPanel renders the line as a plain {line.text} with only a dismiss button (src/components/dashboard/insights-panel.tsx:45) — no navigation to the affected members.
findings.recommendation: Make insights with a known scope clickable (wrap in a Link to the relevant filtered list, e.g. the at-risk members view) while keeping the dismiss control. Keep non-navigable insights as text.

findings.title: InvitePortalButton (and Archive/Contact actions) surface a 403 to managers as a misleading generic 'server error' toast
findings.evidence: src/components/members/invite-portal-button.tsx:46-64 — the error switch handles already_linked/no_email/invalid_email/email_taken/not_found but has NO case for a 403/forbidden; a manager's POST returns {error:'forbidden'} (status 403) from requireAdminContext (src/lib/admin-context.ts:97-101), so it falls into `default → toast.error(t('errors.serverError'))`. ArchiveMemberButton (src/components/members/archive-member-button.tsx:74-79) and ContactActions (src/components/members/contact-actions.tsx:56-64) similarly map any non-ok to a generic/notFound/conflict message — none distinguishes 403 'role-denied'.
findings.recommendation: Preferred: fix finding #1 so these client components are never reachable by managers. If any write affordance must remain visible to managers, add an explicit 403 branch mapping to a 'read-only / contact an admin' message (reuse admin.members.invoices.actions.disabledForManager). Do not let role denials fall through to errors.serverError.

findings.title: Manager sidebar shows the same nav as admin; combined with the detail-page issue there is no role-level signal that the staff surface is read-only
findings.evidence: src/config/nav.ts:34-36 documents that NavItem.roles is 'Type-only for now — filtering logic deferred until a role-differentiated nav item exists', and staffNavConfig (lines 95-238) sets no per-role visibility; src/components/layout/staff-sidebar consumes the config unfiltered. The admin layout (src/app/(staff)/admin/layout.tsx:30-35) only distinguishes member→redirect, not admin vs manager. So a manager sees Plans, Members, Invoices, Broadcasts, Users, Settings, etc. identical to admin.
findings.recommendation: Out of scope for a point-fix, but consider a single persistent 'Read-only' role indicator in the staff header (the UserMenu already receives role at layout.tsx:67) so the constraint is communicated proactively rather than discovered per-action. This is a polish/backlog item, not a launch blocker.

findings.title: Post-onboarding landing is the F1 placeholder dashboard ('F4/F5/F6 coming soon') with no profile-completion or plan/renewal surface
findings.evidence: redeemInvite redirects to portalHomePath → /portal (src/modules/auth/application/redeem-invite.ts:244). /portal (src/app/(member)/portal/page.tsx:48-90) still renders the F1 'roadmap' card listing F4 Invoices, F6 Events, F5 Renewal as future work (all shipped) plus a static info@swecham.se contact card. No 'complete your profile' prompt, no plan/benefits surface, no renewal entry.
findings.recommendation: Replace the stale roadmap card with live entry points (Complete your profile, View your plan & benefits, Pay invoices, Renew). At minimum delete the 'coming soon' roadmap describing already-shipped F4/F5/F6.

findings.title: Invitation dead-link offers no self-service recovery and the page-level dead-token branch lacks the 'contact admin' guidance the form branch has
findings.evidence: On a dead/expired token the page (src/app/(auth-public)/invite/[token]/page.tsx:78-86) renders only `t('errors.tokenExpired')` inside the alert. The form's own link-invalid branch (src/components/auth/invite-redeem-form.tsx:154-164) additionally renders `t('errors.contactAdminCta')` ('contact administrator to request a new link'). The two dead-token surfaces are inconsistent: a token that is dead at page-load gives the member less guidance than one that goes dead at submit.
findings.recommendation: Add `t('errors.contactAdminCta')` to the page-level dead-token alert block so both invitation dead-token surfaces render the same recovery guidance.
